83	// GetEntityDisplayName returns a human-readable label for any supported entity
84	// type by querying the authoritative name column for that table.
85	//
86	//   entity_type   table       column
87	//   -----------   ---------   ------
88	//   user          users       username
89	//   material      materials   title
90	//   course        courses     name
91	//   location      locations   name
92	//
93	// Returns a safe fallback ("<type> #<id>") when the row is not found or the
94	// type is not one of the four above, so callers never need to handle an error.
95	func (r *AdminRepository) GetEntityDisplayName(entityType string, entityID int64) string {
96		var q string
97		switch entityType {
98		case "user":
99			q = `SELECT username FROM users WHERE id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
100		case "material":
101			q = `SELECT title FROM materials WHERE id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
102		case "course":
103			q = `SELECT name FROM courses WHERE id = ?`
104		case "location":
105			q = `SELECT name FROM locations WHERE id = ?`
106		default:
107			return entityType + " #" + strconv.FormatInt(entityID, 10)
108		}
109		var name string
110		if err := r.db.QueryRow(q, entityID).Scan(&name); err != nil {
111			return entityType + " #" + strconv.FormatInt(entityID, 10)
112		}
113		return name
114	}
115	
116	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
117	// Duplicate detection
118	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
119	
120	// DuplicatePair represents two users that may be the same person.
121	type DuplicatePair struct {
122		UserA models.User
123		UserB models.User
124		Score float64 // similarity 0-1
125	}
126	
127	// FindDuplicateUsers returns user pairs that are likely the same person.
128	//
129	// # Semantic mapping — schema fields → specification concepts
130	//
131	// The prompt specifies two signals: "exact ID" and "fuzzy(name, DOB)".
132	//
133	//   Spec concept          → Schema column     Rationale
134	//   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
135	//   Exact identifier (ID) → external_id       Dedicated column for the
136	//                                             institution-issued identifier
137	//                                             (student number, employee ID, etc.).
138	//                                             Two accounts sharing the same
139	//                                             non-null external_id are
140	//                                             definitively the same person.
141	//   Name (fuzzy)          → full_name         Dedicated real-name column.
142	//                                             Falls back to username when
143	//                                             full_name has not been set.
144	//   Date of birth (fuzzy) → date_of_birth     Direct column, exact match only
145	//                                             (birth dates are discrete values).
146	//
147	// # Detection strategies
148	//
149	//  1. Exact identifier match — both accounts share the same non-null external_id
150	//     → score 1.0.
151	//
152	//  2. Fuzzy identity match — composite weighted score for pairs NOT already
153	//     matched by Pass 1:
154	//
155	//       score = 0.6 × name_similarity  +  0.4 × dob_match
156	//
157	//     name_similarity: Levenshtein-based 0–1 ratio on full_name (username fallback).
158	//     dob_match:       1.0 when both records carry the same non-null DOB; 0 otherwise.
159	//
160	//     Threshold = 0.65 — ensures neither signal alone is sufficient:
161	//       - DOB match only  (0.4)     → below threshold, excluded.
162	//       - Name match only (max 0.6) → only identical names reach threshold;
163	//         any divergence requires a DOB match to compensate.
164	//       - Both signals    (≥ 0.65)  → included as probable duplicate.
165	//
166	// SQL pre-filters candidates to pairs sharing the same DOB or the same 4-char
167	// name prefix; Go then applies the full composite formula.
168	// Soft-deleted users are excluded. Results sorted by score desc, capped at limit.
169	func (r *AdminRepository) FindDuplicateUsers(limit int) ([]DuplicatePair, error) {
170		// ---- Pass 1: exact ID — both accounts share the same non-null external_id_idx ----
171		// Uses the HMAC blind index instead of the AES-GCM ciphertext.  Randomized
172		// GCM encryption produces a unique ciphertext on every call, so equality
173		// comparison on the raw external_id column would never match.  The blind index
174		// is deterministic: HMAC-SHA256(derived_key, plaintext), so SQL = works.
175		const exactQ = `
176			SELECT a.id, a.username, a.email, a.password_hash, a.role,
177			       a.failed_attempts, a.locked_until, a.date_of_birth,
178			       a.full_name, a.full_name_idx, a.external_id, a.external_id_idx,
179			       a.created_at, a.updated_at, a.deleted_at, a.must_change_password,
180			       b.id, b.username, b.email, b.password_hash, b.role,
181			       b.failed_attempts, b.locked_until, b.date_of_birth,
182			       b.full_name, b.full_name_idx, b.external_id, b.external_id_idx,
183			       b.created_at, b.updated_at, b.deleted_at, b.must_change_password
184			FROM   users a
185			JOIN   users b ON b.id > a.id
186			             AND a.external_id_idx IS NOT NULL
187			             AND a.external_id_idx = b.external_id_idx
188			WHERE  a.deleted_at IS NULL AND b.deleted_at IS NULL
189			ORDER  BY a.id`
190	
191		exactRows, err := r.db.Query(exactQ)
192		if err != nil {
193			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: FindDuplicateUsers (exact): %w", err)
194		}
195		pairs, err := scanUserPairs(exactRows)
196		if err != nil {
197			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: FindDuplicateUsers (exact scan): %w", err)
198		}
199		for i := range pairs {
200			pairs[i].Score = 1.0
201		}
202	
203		// ---- Pass 2: fuzzy — DOB exact + name blind-index equality ----
204		// Excludes pairs already matched in Pass 1 (same external_id_idx).
205		// The SUBSTR(full_name) pre-filter from the old query is removed: comparing
206		// AES-GCM ciphertexts character-by-character is meaningless.  Instead, the
207		// SQL pre-filter uses:
208		//   a) date_of_birth exact match, OR
209		//   b) full_name_idx equality (deterministic HMAC — same plaintext → same index).
210		// Go-level scoring uses the same signals: blind-index equality → nameSim = 1.0;
211		// username Levenshtein is kept as a fallback for accounts without a full_name.
212		const fuzzyQ = `
213			SELECT a.id, a.username, a.email, a.password_hash, a.role,
214			       a.failed_attempts, a.locked_until, a.date_of_birth,
215			       a.full_name, a.full_name_idx, a.external_id, a.external_id_idx,
216			       a.created_at, a.updated_at, a.deleted_at, a.must_change_password,
217			       b.id, b.username, b.email, b.password_hash, b.role,
218			       b.failed_attempts, b.locked_until, b.date_of_birth,
219			       b.full_name, b.full_name_idx, b.external_id, b.external_id_idx,
220			       b.created_at, b.updated_at, b.deleted_at, b.must_change_password
221			FROM   users a
222			JOIN   users b ON b.id > a.id
223			             AND (a.external_id_idx IS NULL OR b.external_id_idx IS NULL
224			                  OR a.external_id_idx != b.external_id_idx)
225			WHERE  a.deleted_at IS NULL
226			  AND  b.deleted_at IS NULL
227			  AND (
228			        (a.date_of_birth IS NOT NULL AND a.date_of_birth = b.date_of_birth)
229			        OR (a.full_name_idx IS NOT NULL AND a.full_name_idx = b.full_name_idx)
230			      )
231			ORDER  BY a.id`
232	
233		fuzzyRows, err := r.db.Query(fuzzyQ)
234		if err != nil {
235			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: FindDuplicateUsers (fuzzy): %w", err)
236		}
237		candidates, err := scanUserPairs(fuzzyRows)
238		if err != nil {
239			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: FindDuplicateUsers (fuzzy scan): %w", err)
240		}
241	
242		// Score each candidate:
243		//   nameSim: 1.0 when full_name_idx matches (same encrypted plaintext);
244		//            Levenshtein on username as a fallback (accounts without full_name).
245		//   dobMatch: 1.0 when both rows carry the same non-null date_of_birth.
246		const (
247			nameWeight = 0.6
248			dobWeight  = 0.4
249			minScore   = 0.65
250		)
251		for _, p := range candidates {
252			var nameSim float64
253			if p.UserA.FullNameIdx != nil && p.UserB.FullNameIdx != nil &&
254				*p.UserA.FullNameIdx != "" && *p.UserA.FullNameIdx == *p.UserB.FullNameIdx {
255				// Deterministic HMAC equality → plaintext names are identical.
256				nameSim = 1.0
257			} else if p.UserA.FullName == nil && p.UserB.FullName == nil {
258				// Neither account has a full_name: fall back to username similarity.
259				nameSim = usernameSimilarity(p.UserA.Username, p.UserB.Username)
260			}
261			// dob_match: 1.0 when both users share an identical, non-null birth date.
262			var dobMatch float64
263			if p.UserA.DateOfBirth != nil && p.UserB.DateOfBirth != nil &&
264				*p.UserA.DateOfBirth == *p.UserB.DateOfBirth {
265				dobMatch = 1.0
266			}
267			score := nameWeight*nameSim + dobWeight*dobMatch
268			if score >= minScore {
269				p.Score = score
270				pairs = append(pairs, p)
271			}
272		}
273	
274		// Sort by score descending, apply limit.
275		sortDuplicatePairs(pairs)
276		if limit > 0 && len(pairs) > limit {
277			pairs = pairs[:limit]
278		}
279		return pairs, nil
280	}
281	
282	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
283	// Duplicate-detection helpers
284	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
285	
286	// scanUserPairs scans rows produced by the self-join queries in FindDuplicateUsers.
287	// Column order must match the SELECT lists in exactQ and fuzzyQ exactly.
288	func scanUserPairs(rows *sql.Rows) ([]DuplicatePair, error) {
289		defer rows.Close()
290		var pairs []DuplicatePair
291		for rows.Next() {
292			var p DuplicatePair
293			a, b := &p.UserA, &p.UserB
294			if err := rows.Scan(
295				&a.ID, &a.Username, &a.Email, &a.PasswordHash, &a.Role,
296				&a.FailedAttempts, &a.LockedUntil, &a.DateOfBirth,
297				&a.FullName, &a.FullNameIdx, &a.ExternalID, &a.ExternalIDIdx,
298				&a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt, &a.DeletedAt, &a.MustChangePassword,
299				&b.ID, &b.Username, &b.Email, &b.PasswordHash, &b.Role,
300				&b.FailedAttempts, &b.LockedUntil, &b.DateOfBirth,
301				&b.FullName, &b.FullNameIdx, &b.ExternalID, &b.ExternalIDIdx,
302				&b.CreatedAt, &b.UpdatedAt, &b.DeletedAt, &b.MustChangePassword,
303			); err != nil {
304				return nil, err
305			}
306			pairs = append(pairs, p)
307		}
308		return pairs, rows.Err()
309	}
310	
311	// usernameSimilarity returns a 0–1 Levenshtein-based similarity score for two
312	// usernames (case-insensitive).
313	func usernameSimilarity(a, b string) float64 {
314		a = strings.ToLower(a)
315		b = strings.ToLower(b)
316		maxLen := len(a)
317		if len(b) > maxLen {
318			maxLen = len(b)
319		}
320		if maxLen == 0 {
321			return 1.0
322		}
323		dist := levenshtein(a, b)
324		return 1.0 - float64(dist)/float64(maxLen)
325	}
326	
327	// levenshtein computes the edit distance between two strings using the
328	// standard dynamic-programming approach.
329	func levenshtein(a, b string) int {
330		ra, rb := []rune(a), []rune(b)
331		la, lb := len(ra), len(rb)
332		if la == 0 {
333			return lb
334		}
335		if lb == 0 {
336			return la
337		}
338		// Use two rows of the DP table to save memory.
339		prev := make([]int, lb+1)
340		curr := make([]int, lb+1)
341		for j := 0; j <= lb; j++ {
342			prev[j] = j
343		}
344		for i := 1; i <= la; i++ {
345			curr[0] = i
346			for j := 1; j <= lb; j++ {
347				cost := 1
348				if ra[i-1] == rb[j-1] {
349					cost = 0
350				}
351				curr[j] = minInt(
352					curr[j-1]+1,        // insertion
353					prev[j]+1,          // deletion
354					prev[j-1]+cost,     // substitution
355				)
356			}
357			prev, curr = curr, prev
358		}
359		return prev[lb]
360	}
361	
362	func minInt(a, b, c int) int {
363		if a < b {
364			if a < c {
365				return a
366			}
367			return c
368		}
369		if b < c {
370			return b
371		}
372		return c
373	}
374	
375	// sortDuplicatePairs sorts pairs by Score descending (insertion sort — small N).
376	func sortDuplicatePairs(pairs []DuplicatePair) {
377		for i := 1; i < len(pairs); i++ {
378			key := pairs[i]
379			j := i - 1
380			for j >= 0 && pairs[j].Score < key.Score {
381				pairs[j+1] = pairs[j]
382				j--
383			}
384			pairs[j+1] = key
385		}
386	}
387	
388	// MergeUsers merges duplicateID into primaryID:
389	//  1. Re-parents orders, ratings, comments, favorites_lists, notifications.
390	//  2. Inserts an entity_duplicates record.
391	//  3. Soft-deletes the duplicate user.
392	func (r *AdminRepository) MergeUsers(primaryID, duplicateID int64, mergedBy int64) error {
393		tx, err := r.db.Begin()
394		if err != nil {
395			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: begin tx: %w", err)
396		}
397		defer tx.Rollback() //nolint:errcheck
398	
399		// 1a. Re-parent orders.
400		if _, err := tx.Exec(`UPDATE orders SET user_id = ? WHERE user_id = ?`, primaryID, duplicateID); err != nil {
401			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: orders: %w", err)
402		}
403	
404		// 1b. Re-parent ratings (ignore conflicts — primary already has a rating).
405		if _, err := tx.Exec(`
406			UPDATE OR IGNORE ratings SET user_id = ? WHERE user_id = ?`,
407			primaryID, duplicateID); err != nil {
408			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: ratings: %w", err)
409		}
410	
411		// 1c. Re-parent comments.
412		if _, err := tx.Exec(`UPDATE comments SET user_id = ? WHERE user_id = ?`, primaryID, duplicateID); err != nil {
413			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: comments: %w", err)
414		}
415	
416		// 1d. Re-parent favorites_lists.
417		if _, err := tx.Exec(`UPDATE favorites_lists SET user_id = ? WHERE user_id = ?`, primaryID, duplicateID); err != nil {
418			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: favorites_lists: %w", err)
419		}
420	
421		// 1e. Re-parent notifications.
422		if _, err := tx.Exec(`UPDATE notifications SET user_id = ? WHERE user_id = ?`, primaryID, duplicateID); err != nil {
423			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: notifications: %w", err)
424		}
425	
426		// 2. Insert entity_duplicates record.
427		const insertDupQ = `
428			INSERT INTO entity_duplicates
429			            (entity_type, primary_id, duplicate_id, status, merged_by, merged_at)
430			VALUES      ('user', ?, ?, 'merged', ?, datetime('now'))`
431		if _, err := tx.Exec(insertDupQ, primaryID, duplicateID, mergedBy); err != nil {
432			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: insert entity_duplicates: %w", err)
433		}
434	
435		// 3. Soft-delete the duplicate.
436		const softDeleteQ = `
437			UPDATE users
438			SET    deleted_at = datetime('now'),
439			       updated_at = datetime('now')
440			WHERE  id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
441		if _, err := tx.Exec(softDeleteQ, duplicateID); err != nil {
442			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: soft-delete duplicate: %w", err)
443		}
444	
445		return tx.Commit()
446	}
447	
448	// GetMergeHistory returns the most-recent limit entity_duplicates records.
449	func (r *AdminRepository) GetMergeHistory(limit int) ([]models.EntityDuplicate, error) {
450		const q = `
451			SELECT id, entity_type, primary_id, duplicate_id, status, merged_by, merged_at
452			FROM   entity_duplicates
453			ORDER  BY merged_at DESC
454			LIMIT  ?`
455	
456		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, limit)
457		if err != nil {
458			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetMergeHistory: %w", err)
459		}
460		defer rows.Close()

462		var out []models.EntityDuplicate

468			); err != nil {

470			}
471			out = append(out, e)
472		}
473		return out, rows.Err()
474	}

480	// WriteAuditLog inserts a row into the audit_log table.
481	func (r *AdminRepository) WriteAuditLog(actorID int64, action, entityType string, entityID int64, before, after interface{}, ip string) error {
482		var beforeJSON, afterJSON *string
483		if before != nil {
484			b, err := json.Marshal(before)
485			if err == nil {
486				s := string(b)
487				beforeJSON = &s
488			}
489		}
490		if after != nil {
491			b, err := json.Marshal(after)
492			if err == nil {
493				s := string(b)
494				afterJSON = &s
495			}
496		}
497		var ipPtr *string
498		if ip != "" {
499			ipPtr = &ip
500		}
501	
502		const q = `
503			INSERT INTO audit_log (actor_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, before_data, after_data, ip, created_at)
504			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`
505		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, actorID, action, entityType, entityID, beforeJSON, afterJSON, ipPtr)
506		if err != nil {
507			return fmt.Errorf("repository: WriteAuditLog: %w", err)
508		}
509		return nil
510	}

77	// OrdersByStatus returns a map of order-status → count for all non-deleted orders.
78	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) OrdersByStatus() (map[string]int, error) {
79		const q = `
80			SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS cnt
81			FROM   orders
82			GROUP  BY status`
83	
84		rows, err := r.db.Query(q)
85		if err != nil {
86			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: OrdersByStatus: %w", err)
87		}
88		defer rows.Close()
89	
90		out := make(map[string]int)
91		for rows.Next() {
92			var status string
93			var cnt int
94			if err := rows.Scan(&status, &cnt); err != nil {
95				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: OrdersByStatus scan: %w", err)
96			}
97			out[status] = cnt
98		}
99		return out, rows.Err()
100	}

102	// periodExpr converts a period string ("7d", "30d", "90d") to a SQLite
103	// datetime modifier.  Defaults to 30 days.
104	func periodExpr(period string) string {
105		switch period {
106		case "7d":
107			return "-7 days"
108		case "90d":
109			return "-90 days"
110		default:
111			return "-30 days"
112		}
113	}
114	
115	// FulfillmentRate returns the fraction of orders (in the given period) whose
116	// status is "completed", expressed as a value 0–100.
117	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) FulfillmentRate(period string) (float64, error) {
118		mod := periodExpr(period)
119		const q = `
120			SELECT
121				CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'completed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) /
122				NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) * 100
123			FROM orders
124			WHERE created_at >= datetime('now', ?)`
125	
126		var rate sql.NullFloat64
127		if err := r.db.QueryRow(q, mod).Scan(&rate); err != nil {
128			return 0, fmt.Errorf("analytics: FulfillmentRate: %w", err)
129		}
130		if !rate.Valid {
131			return 0, nil
132		}
133		return rate.Float64, nil
134	}
135	
136	// ReturnRate returns the fraction of completed orders (in the given period)
137	// that have at least one return request, expressed as a value 0–100.
138	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) ReturnRate(period string) (float64, error) {
139		mod := periodExpr(period)
140		const q = `
141			SELECT
142				CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT rr.order_id) AS REAL) /
143				NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT o.id), 0) * 100
144			FROM   orders o
145			LEFT   JOIN return_requests rr ON rr.order_id = o.id
146			WHERE  o.status = 'completed'
147			  AND  o.created_at >= datetime('now', ?)`
148	
149		var rate sql.NullFloat64
150		if err := r.db.QueryRow(q, mod).Scan(&rate); err != nil {
151			return 0, fmt.Errorf("analytics: ReturnRate: %w", err)
152		}
153		if !rate.Valid {
154			return 0, nil
155		}
156		return rate.Float64, nil
157	}

76	END;

79	AFTER UPDATE ON materials BEGIN

81	    VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.title, old.author, old.subject);

200	// their average star rating.
201	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) TopMaterials(limit int) ([]MaterialStat, error) {
202		const q = `
203			SELECT m.id,
204			       m.title,
205			       COUNT(oi.id)                                    AS order_count,
206			       COALESCE(AVG(CAST(rt.stars AS REAL)), 0.0)     AS avg_rating
207			FROM   materials m
208			JOIN   order_items oi ON oi.material_id = m.id
209			LEFT   JOIN ratings rt ON rt.material_id = m.id
210			WHERE  m.deleted_at IS NULL
211			GROUP  BY m.id, m.title
212			ORDER  BY order_count DESC
213			LIMIT  ?`
214	
215		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, limit)
216		if err != nil {
217			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: TopMaterials: %w", err)
218		}
219		defer rows.Close()
220	
221		var out []MaterialStat
222		for rows.Next() {
223			var s MaterialStat
224			if err := rows.Scan(&s.MaterialID, &s.Title, &s.OrderCount, &s.AvgRating); err != nil {
225				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: TopMaterials scan: %w", err)
226			}
227			out = append(out, s)
228		}
229		return out, rows.Err()
230	}

236	// SaveKPISnapshot inserts a new kpi_snapshots row.
237	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) SaveKPISnapshot(name, dimension string, value float64, period string) error {
238		const q = `
239			INSERT INTO kpi_snapshots (metric_name, dimension, value, period, computed_at)
240			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`
241	
242		var dimPtr *string
243		if dimension != "" {
244			dimPtr = &dimension
245		}
246		var periodPtr *string
247		if period != "" {
248			periodPtr = &period
249		}
250	
251		if _, err := r.db.Exec(q, name, dimPtr, value, periodPtr); err != nil {
252			return fmt.Errorf("analytics: SaveKPISnapshot: %w", err)
253		}
254		return nil
255	}

257	// GetKPIHistory returns the most-recent `limit` snapshots for the given metric
258	// and dimension, ordered newest-first.
259	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) GetKPIHistory(name, dimension string, limit int) ([]models.KPISnapshot, error) {
260		const q = `
261			SELECT id, metric_name, dimension, value, period, computed_at
262			FROM   kpi_snapshots
263			WHERE  metric_name = ?
264			  AND  (dimension = ? OR (dimension IS NULL AND ? = ''))
265			ORDER  BY computed_at DESC
266			LIMIT  ?`
267	
268		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, name, dimension, dimension, limit)
269		if err != nil {
270			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: GetKPIHistory: %w", err)
271		}
272		defer rows.Close()
273	
274		var out []models.KPISnapshot
275		for rows.Next() {
276			var k models.KPISnapshot
277			if err := rows.Scan(&k.ID, &k.MetricName, &k.Dimension, &k.Value, &k.Period, &k.ComputedAt); err != nil {
278				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: GetKPIHistory scan: %w", err)
279			}
280			out = append(out, k)
281		}
282		return out, rows.Err()
283	}

285	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
286	// Instructor: course order stats
287	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
288	
289	// CourseOrderStats returns per-section/material demand and fulfillment data
290	// for all course plans owned by the given instructor.  Results come from the
291	// actual course_plans, course_sections, courses, and order_items tables — no
292	// approximations.
293	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) CourseOrderStats(instructorID int64) ([]CourseOrderStat, error) {
294		const q = `
295			SELECT
296				c.name                                                                AS course_name,
297				COALESCE(cs.name, '')                                                 AS section_name,
298				m.title                                                               AS material_title,
299				cp.requested_qty,
300				cp.approved_qty,
301				cp.status                                                             AS plan_status,
302				COUNT(oi.id)                                                          AS ordered,
303				SUM(CASE WHEN oi.fulfillment_status = 'fulfilled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fulfilled
304			FROM   courses c
305			JOIN   course_plans cp ON cp.course_id = c.id
306			LEFT   JOIN course_sections cs ON cs.id = cp.section_id
307			JOIN   materials m ON m.id = cp.material_id
308			LEFT   JOIN order_items oi ON oi.material_id = m.id
309			LEFT   JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id AND o.status != 'canceled'
310			WHERE  c.instructor_id = ?
311			  AND  m.deleted_at IS NULL
312			GROUP  BY c.id, COALESCE(cp.section_id, 0), cp.material_id
313			ORDER  BY c.name, COALESCE(cs.name, ''), m.title`
314	
315		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, instructorID)
316		if err != nil {
317			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: CourseOrderStats: %w", err)
318		}
319		defer rows.Close()
320	
321		var out []CourseOrderStat
322		for rows.Next() {
323			var s CourseOrderStat
324			if err := rows.Scan(&s.CourseName, &s.SectionName, &s.MaterialTitle,
325				&s.RequestedQty, &s.ApprovedQty, &s.PlanStatus,
326				&s.Ordered, &s.Fulfilled); err != nil {
327				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: CourseOrderStats scan: %w", err)
328			}
329			out = append(out, s)
330		}
331		return out, rows.Err()
332	}

334	// CountPendingPlanItems returns the number of course_plans rows with
335	// status='pending' across all courses owned by the given instructor.
336	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) CountPendingPlanItems(instructorID int64) (int, error) {
337		const q = `
338			SELECT COUNT(*)
339			FROM   course_plans cp
340			JOIN   courses c ON c.id = cp.course_id
341			WHERE  c.instructor_id = ? AND cp.status = 'pending'`
342		var n int
343		if err := r.db.QueryRow(q, instructorID).Scan(&n); err != nil {
344			return 0, fmt.Errorf("analytics: CountPendingPlanItems: %w", err)
345		}
346		return n, nil
347	}

373				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: GetLocations scan: %w", err)

405	// UpsertSpatialAggregate inserts or replaces a spatial_aggregates row.
406	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) UpsertSpatialAggregate(layerType, cellKey, metric string, value float64) error {
407		const q = `
408			INSERT INTO spatial_aggregates (layer_type, cell_key, metric, value, computed_at)
409			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
410			ON CONFLICT(layer_type, cell_key, metric)
411			DO UPDATE SET value = excluded.value, computed_at = excluded.computed_at`
412	
413		if _, err := r.db.Exec(q, layerType, cellKey, metric, value); err != nil {
414			return fmt.Errorf("analytics: UpsertSpatialAggregate: %w", err)
415		}
416		return nil
417	}
418	
419	// ComputeGridAggregation groups locations of the given layerType into lat/lng
420	// grid cells of size gridSizeKm (approximated as degrees: 1 deg ≈ 111 km),
421	// counts the locations per cell, and saves the result to spatial_aggregates.
422	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) ComputeGridAggregation(layerType, metric string, gridSizeKm float64) error {
423		locs, err := r.GetLocations(layerType)
424		if err != nil {
425			return fmt.Errorf("analytics: ComputeGridAggregation: fetch locations: %w", err)
426		}
427	
428		const degPerKm = 1.0 / 111.0
429		gridStep := gridSizeKm * degPerKm
430	
431		counts := make(map[string]float64)
432		for _, loc := range locs {
433			if loc.Lat == nil || loc.Lng == nil {
434				continue
435			}
436			cellLat := math.Floor(*loc.Lat/gridStep) * gridStep
437			cellLng := math.Floor(*loc.Lng/gridStep) * gridStep
438			cellKey := fmt.Sprintf("%.6f,%.6f", cellLat, cellLng)
439			counts[cellKey]++
440		}
441	
442		for cellKey, count := range counts {
443			if err := r.UpsertSpatialAggregate(layerType, cellKey, metric, count); err != nil {
444				return fmt.Errorf("analytics: ComputeGridAggregation: upsert %q: %w", cellKey, err)
445			}
446		}
447		return nil
448	}

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# run_tests.sh — Execute all test suites and print a pass/fail summary.
3	#
4	# Usage:
5	#   ./run_tests.sh            # run all suites
6	#   ./run_tests.sh -v         # verbose (stream test output)
7	#   ./run_tests.sh -race      # enable Go race detector
8	#   ./run_tests.sh -v -race   # both
9	
10	set -euo pipefail
11	
12	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
13	# Colour helpers
14	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
15	GREEN="\033[0;32m"
16	RED="\033[0;31m"
17	YELLOW="\033[0;33m"
18	RESET="\033[0m"
19	
20	pass() { echo -e "${GREEN}PASS${RESET}  $1"; }
21	fail() { echo -e "${RED}FAIL${RESET}  $1"; }
22	info() { echo -e "${YELLOW}----${RESET}  $1"; }
23	
24	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25	# Parse flags
26	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
27	VERBOSE=""
28	RACE=""
29	for arg in "$@"; do
30	  case "$arg" in
31	    -v)     VERBOSE="-v" ;;
32	    -race)  RACE="-race" ;;
33	    *)      echo "Unknown flag: $arg"; exit 1 ;;
34	  esac
35	done
36	
37	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	# Locate the project root (the directory containing this script).
39	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
41	cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
42	
43	# Export the migration path so test DBs can find the schema regardless of the
44	# working directory from which tests run.
45	export MIGRATION_PATH="$SCRIPT_DIR/migrations/001_schema.sql"
46	
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	# Suite definitions: (label, package_path)
49	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	declare -a SUITE_LABELS=(
51	  "Unit tests (state machine, inventory, auth, validation, rate-limiter)"
52	  "API functional tests (normal inputs, bad params, permission errors)"
53	  "Integration tests — auth & session"
54	  "Integration tests — materials, comments, favorites"
55	  "Integration tests — orders & fulfillment"
56	  "Integration tests — distribution & ledger"
57	  "Integration tests — messaging & inbox"
58	  "Integration tests — moderation queue"
59	  "Integration tests — admin panel"
60	)
61	declare -a SUITE_PKGS=(
62	  "./unit_tests/..."
63	  "./API_tests/..."
64	  "./internal/integration/"
65	  "./internal/integration/"
66	  "./internal/integration/"
67	  "./internal/integration/"
68	  "./internal/integration/"
69	  "./internal/integration/"
70	  "./internal/integration/"
71	)
72	# Optional test-name filter per suite (empty = no filter, runs all).
73	declare -a SUITE_RUN=(
74	  ""
75	  ""
76	  "TestAuth|TestHealth|TestLogin|TestLogout|TestRegister"
77	  "TestMaterial|TestAddComment|TestReport|TestFavorite|TestShareLink|TestRate"
78	  "TestPlaceOrder|TestOrderDetail|TestOrderCancel|TestConfirmPayment|TestReturn"
79	  "TestDistribution|TestIssue|TestReturn|TestExchange|TestReissue|TestLedger|TestCustody"
80	  "TestMessag|TestInbox|TestDND|TestSubscri|TestBadge"
81	  "TestModeration|TestApprove|TestRemove"
82	  "TestAdmin"
83	)
84	
85	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	# Run each suite and collect results.
87	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
88	TOTAL=0
89	PASSED=0
90	FAILED=0
91	FAILED_SUITES=()
92	
93	echo ""
94	echo "========================================================"
95	echo " w2t86 — Test Runner"
96	echo " $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')"
97	echo "========================================================"
98	echo ""
99	
100	for i in "${!SUITE_LABELS[@]}"; do
101	  label="${SUITE_LABELS[$i]}"
102	  pkg="${SUITE_PKGS[$i]}"
103	  run_filter="${SUITE_RUN[$i]}"
104	
105	  TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
106	  info "Running: $label"
107	
108	  # Build the go test command.
109	  cmd=(go test -tags sqlite_fts5 $RACE $VERBOSE -timeout 120s)
110	  if [[ -n "$run_filter" ]]; then
111	    cmd+=(-run "$run_filter")
112	  fi
113	  cmd+=("$pkg")
114	
115	  # Capture output; on failure print it.
116	  tmp=$(mktemp)
117	  if "${cmd[@]}" >"$tmp" 2>&1; then
118	    pass "$label"
119	    PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
120	    # In verbose mode the output was already streamed; otherwise stay quiet.
121	    if [[ -n "$VERBOSE" ]]; then
122	      cat "$tmp"
123	    fi
124	  else
125	    fail "$label"
126	    FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
127	    FAILED_SUITES+=("$label")
128	    # Always show output on failure.
129	    cat "$tmp"
130	  fi
131	  rm -f "$tmp"
132	  echo ""
133	done
134	
135	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
136	# Summary
137	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
138	echo "========================================================"
139	echo " Results: ${PASSED}/${TOTAL} suites passed"
140	echo "========================================================"
141	
142	if [[ $FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
143	  echo ""
144	  echo -e "${RED}Failed suites:${RESET}"
145	  for s in "${FAILED_SUITES[@]}"; do
146	    echo -e "  ${RED}✘${RESET} $s"
147	  done
148	  echo ""
149	  exit 1
150	else
151	  echo ""
152	  echo -e "${GREEN}All suites passed.${RESET}"
153	  echo ""
154	  exit 0
155	fi
156

37	// TestCreateUser verifies POST /admin/users creates a user and redirects to the
38	// new user's profile page.
39	func TestCreateUser(t *testing.T) {
40		app, db, cleanup := newTestApp(t)
41		defer cleanup()
42	
43		adminCookie := loginAs(t, app, db, "admin")
44	
45		body := "username=newstudent123&email=newstudent123@example.com&password=SuperSecurePass1!&role=student"
46		resp := makeRequest(app, http.MethodPost, "/admin/users",
47			body, adminCookie, "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
48	
49		// On success: redirects to /admin/users/:id (302).
50		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound {
51			t.Fatalf("expected 302 on create user, got %d; body: %s",
52				resp.StatusCode, readBody(resp))
53		}
54	
55		if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusFound {
56			loc := resp.Header.Get("Location")
57			if !strings.HasPrefix(loc, "/admin/users/") {
58				t.Errorf("expected redirect to /admin/users/:id, got: %s", loc)
59			}
60		}
61	
62		// Verify DB.
63		var count int
64		if err := db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE username = 'newstudent123'`).Scan(&count); err != nil {
65			t.Fatalf("query user: %v", err)
66		}
67		if count != 1 {
68			t.Errorf("expected 1 user row for newstudent123, got %d", count)
69		}
70	}

72	// TestUpdateRole verifies POST /admin/users/:id/role changes a user's role.
73	func TestUpdateRole(t *testing.T) {
74		app, db, cleanup := newTestApp(t)
75		defer cleanup()
76	
77		targetUser := createTestUser(t, db, "student")
78		adminCookie := loginAs(t, app, db, "admin")
79	
80		body := "role=instructor"
81		resp := makeRequest(app, http.MethodPost,
82			fmt.Sprintf("/admin/users/%d/role", targetUser.ID),
83			body, adminCookie, "application/x-www-form-urlencoded", htmxHeaders())
84	
85		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound {
86			t.Fatalf("expected 200 or 302 on update role, got %d; body: %s",
87				resp.StatusCode, readBody(resp))
88		}
89	
90		// Verify DB.
91		var role string
92		if err := db.QueryRow(`SELECT role FROM users WHERE id = ?`, targetUser.ID).Scan(&role); err != nil {
93			t.Fatalf("query user role: %v", err)
94		}
95		if role != "instructor" {
96			t.Errorf("expected role 'instructor', got %q", role)
97		}
98	}

100	// TestUnlockUser verifies POST /admin/users/:id/unlock clears the lock on a user.
101	func TestUnlockUser(t *testing.T) {
102		app, db, cleanup := newTestApp(t)
103		defer cleanup()
104	
105		targetUser := createTestUser(t, db, "student")
106	
107		// Lock the user directly.
108		if _, err := db.Exec(`UPDATE users SET locked_until = datetime('now', '+15 minutes'), failed_attempts = 5 WHERE id = ?`, targetUser.ID); err != nil {
109			t.Fatalf("lock user: %v", err)
110		}
111	
112		adminCookie := loginAs(t, app, db, "admin")
113	
114		resp := makeRequest(app, http.MethodPost,
115			fmt.Sprintf("/admin/users/%d/unlock", targetUser.ID),
116			"", adminCookie, "application/x-www-form-urlencoded", htmxHeaders())
117	
118		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound {
119			t.Fatalf("expected 200 or 302 on unlock user, got %d; body: %s",
120				resp.StatusCode, readBody(resp))
121		}
122	
123		// Verify DB.
124		var lockedUntil *string
125		var failedAttempts int
126		if err := db.QueryRow(`SELECT locked_until, failed_attempts FROM users WHERE id = ?`, targetUser.ID).Scan(&lockedUntil, &failedAttempts); err != nil {
127			t.Fatalf("query user: %v", err)
128		}
129		if lockedUntil != nil {
130			t.Errorf("expected locked_until to be NULL after unlock")
131		}
132		if failedAttempts != 0 {
133			t.Errorf("expected failed_attempts=0 after unlock, got %d", failedAttempts)
134		}
135	}

1	package models
2	
3	// models.go — Go structs for every table in migrations/001_schema.sql.
4	//
5	// Conventions:
6	//   - All IDs are int64 (maps to SQLite INTEGER PRIMARY KEY).
7	//   - Nullable TEXT columns → *string.
8	//   - Nullable REAL / INTEGER columns that may be NULL in the DB → *float64 / *int.
9	//   - Timestamp columns are stored as ISO-8601 TEXT; represented as *string so
10	//     that NULL is preserved without importing time in every layer.
11	//   - db struct tags match column names exactly.
12	
13	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
14	// Users & Auth
15	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
16	
17	// User represents a row in the users table.
18	type User struct {
19		ID                 int64   `db:"id"`
20		Username           string  `db:"username"`
21		Email              string  `db:"email"`
22		PasswordHash       string  `db:"password_hash"`
23		Role               string  `db:"role"`
24		FailedAttempts     int     `db:"failed_attempts"`
25		LockedUntil        *string `db:"locked_until"`
26		DateOfBirth        *string `db:"date_of_birth"`
27		FullName           *string `db:"full_name"`           // real name; AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest
28		FullNameIdx        *string `db:"full_name_idx"`       // HMAC blind index for duplicate detection
29		ExternalID         *string `db:"external_id"`         // institution-issued ID; AES-256-GCM encrypted
30		ExternalIDIdx      *string `db:"external_id_idx"`     // HMAC blind index for duplicate detection
31		CreatedAt          string  `db:"created_at"`
32		UpdatedAt          string  `db:"updated_at"`
33		DeletedAt          *string `db:"deleted_at"`
34		MustChangePassword int     `db:"must_change_password"` // 1 = redirect to password reset on next login
35	}
36	
37	// GetID returns the user's ID, satisfying the observability hasID interface
38	// used by the request logger to correlate user IDs in log output.
39	func (u *User) GetID() int64 { return u.ID }
40	
41	// Session represents a row in the sessions table.
42	type Session struct {
43		ID        int64  `db:"id"`
44		UserID    int64  `db:"user_id"`
45		TokenHash string `db:"token_hash"`
46		ExpiresAt string `db:"expires_at"`
47		CreatedAt string `db:"created_at"`
48	}
49	
50	// UserCustomField is an alias for EntityCustomField retained for backwards
51	// compatibility with existing handler/service signatures.
52	type UserCustomField = EntityCustomField
53

55	type EntityCustomField struct {

1	# District Materials Commerce & Logistics Portal
2	
3	A web-based portal for managing district-wide distribution of educational materials. It provides role-aware workflows for students (browsing, ordering, favorites), instructors (course plans, approvals), clerks (distribution, ledger), moderators (comment queue), and administrators (users, analytics, settings). Built with Go, Fiber, SQLite, HTMX, and Alpine.js.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Go 1.22 or later
8	- GCC (required to compile the `mattn/go-sqlite3` CGo driver)
9	  - macOS: `xcode-select --install`
10	  - Ubuntu/Debian: `sudo apt install gcc`
11	  - Windows: install [TDM-GCC](https://jmeubank.github.io/tdm-gcc/) or use WSL
12	
13	## Setup
14	
15	1. **Clone the repository**
16	
17	   ```bash
18	   git clone <repo-url>
19	   cd w2t86
20	   ```
21	
22	2. **Install Go dependencies**
23	
24	   ```bash
25	   go mod tidy
26	   ```
27	
28	3. **Download HTMX and Alpine.js**
29	
30	   Place the minified files in `web/static/js/`:
31	
32	   ```bash
33	   # HTMX 2.x
34	   curl -Lo web/static/js/htmx.min.js \
35	     https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2/dist/htmx.min.js
36	
37	   # Alpine.js 3.x
38	   curl -Lo web/static/js/alpine.min.js \
39	     https://unpkg.com/alpinejs@3/dist/cdn.min.js
40	   ```
41	
42	4. **Configure environment**
43	
44	   Create a `.env` file in the project root (it is gitignored — never commit it):
45	
46	   ```bash
47	   # Generate the two required secrets
48	   echo "ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
49	   echo "SESSION_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
50	
51	   # Add the remaining variables with your preferred values
52	   echo "PORT=3000"            >> .env
53	   echo "DB_PATH=data/portal.db" >> .env
54	   echo "APP_ENV=development"  >> .env
55	   echo "BANNED_WORDS="        >> .env
56	   ```
57	
58	   See the [Environment Variables](#environment-variables) section below for a
59	   description of every variable.
60	
61	5. **Run the server**
62	
63	   ```bash
64	   go run -tags sqlite_fts5 ./cmd/server
65	   ```
66	
67	   The server listens on `http://localhost:3000` by default.
68	
69	## Docker
70	
71	### Quick start
72	```bash
73	# Create .env with generated secrets (gitignored — never commit this file)
74	echo "ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >  .env
75	echo "SESSION_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
76	echo "PORT=3000"             >> .env
77	echo "DB_PATH=/app/data/portal.db" >> .env
78	echo "APP_ENV=production"    >> .env
79	
80	docker compose up -d
81	```
82	
83	### Development (with live template reload)
84	```bash
85	docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml up
86	```
87	
88	### Useful commands
89	```bash
90	make docker-logs     # tail logs
91	make docker-down     # stop
92	make test            # run tests locally
93	```
94	
95	## Environment Variables
96	
97	The application reads all configuration from environment variables.
98	A **template `.env` file** is committed to the repository with placeholder
99	values only — no real secrets are stored in it.  Copy it to a local `.env`,
100	replace the placeholder values, and the file will be ignored by git.
101	
102	### Minimal `.env` for local development
103	
104	```dotenv
105	# --- Required secrets (generate with: openssl rand -hex 32) ---
106	ENCRYPTION_KEY=<64-hex-char string, 32 bytes>
107	SESSION_SECRET=<long random string>
108	
109	# --- Optional: shown here with their default values ---
110	PORT=3000
111	DB_PATH=data/portal.db
112	APP_ENV=development
113	BANNED_WORDS=
114	```
115	
116	### Variable reference
117	
118	| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
119	|---|---|---|---|
120	| `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | **yes** | — | 64-character hex string (32 bytes) used as the AES-256-GCM key for encrypting sensitive user custom fields. Generate with `openssl rand -hex 32`. |
121	| `SESSION_SECRET` | **yes** | — | Arbitrary secret string used to sign and verify session tokens. Use a long random value; `openssl rand -hex 32` is sufficient. |
122	| `PORT` | no | `3000` | TCP port the HTTP server binds to. |
123	| `DB_PATH` | no | `data/portal.db` | Filesystem path to the SQLite database file. The parent directory is created automatically on first run. In Docker the volume is mounted at `/app/data`, so use `/app/data/portal.db`. |
124	| `APP_ENV` | no | `development` | Runtime environment. Set to `production` to disable template hot-reload and enable stricter security defaults. Any other value is treated as development. |
125	| `BANNED_WORDS` | no | *(empty)* | Comma-separated list of words blocked in material comments (e.g. `spam,abuse`). Leave empty to disable the filter entirely. |
126	
127	## Default Credentials
128	
129	| Username | Password       | Role  |
130	|----------|----------------|-------|
131	| `admin`  | `ChangeMe123!` | admin |
132	
133	**Change the admin password immediately after first login.**
134	
135	The default admin account is inserted by the initial migration. Update it via the Admin Settings page or directly in the database.
136	
137	## Available Roles
138	
139	| Role         | Capabilities                                                        |
140	|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
141	| `student`    | Browse materials, place orders, manage favorites, inbox             |
142	| `instructor` | Course plans, approve orders, inbox                                 |
143	| `clerk`      | Distribution events, ledger, backorder management, inbox            |
144	| `moderator`  | Review and act on reported comments, inbox                          |
145	| `admin`      | Full access: user management, analytics, all settings, all of above |
146

1	package integration_test
2	
3	// Integration tests for the w2t86 portal.
4	//
5	// Template rendering notes:
6	//   - The Fiber app is built WITH the html template engine pointing to
7	//     ../../web/templates (relative to this package).  Most handlers call
8	//     c.Render, so we need the engine wired up.
9	//   - For handlers that are templated but also have HTMX paths that return
10	//     plain text/JSON, we send HX-Request: true to get text responses.
11	//   - For handlers whose success path is a redirect (302/303), we test just
12	//     the redirect status code — no template is rendered.
13	//   - For handlers that render a full-page template on success, tests assert
14	//     exactly http.StatusOK (200). A 500 response always indicates a real bug
15	//     and must never be treated as acceptable.
16	//
17	// IMPORTANT: Fiber v2 with multiple app.Group("", mw...) shares middleware
18	// across ALL requests because the empty prefix makes the group middleware global.
19	// To avoid cross-group RBAC interference, we use per-route middleware by
20	// passing the RBAC handler directly as an additional argument to each route
21	// registration instead of using groups with empty prefix for RBAC.
22	
23	import (
24		"database/sql"
25		"fmt"
26		"io"
27		"net/http"
28		"net/http/httptest"
29		"strings"
30		"testing"
31		"time"
32	
33		"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
34		fibercsrf "github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/csrf"
35		"github.com/gofiber/template/html/v2"
36	
37		"w2t86/internal/config"
38		"w2t86/internal/handlers"
39		"w2t86/internal/middleware"
40		"w2t86/internal/models"
41		"w2t86/internal/repository"
42		"w2t86/internal/services"
43		"w2t86/internal/testutil"
44	)
45	
46	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
47	// TestApp — builds a complete wired Fiber app backed by an in-memory SQLite DB.
48	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
49	
50	// newTestApp builds the full Fiber app wired exactly like main.go but using an
51	// in-memory test DB and a template engine pointing to ../../web/templates.
52	// It returns the app, the underlying DB, and a cleanup function.
53	//
54	// RBAC is applied per-route (not via empty-prefix groups) to avoid Fiber v2's
55	// behavior of running ALL group middlewares for ALL requests when the group
56	// prefix is "".
57	func newTestApp(t *testing.T) (*fiber.App, *sql.DB, func()) {
58		t.Helper()
59	
60		db := testutil.NewTestDB(t)
61	
62		// Wire repositories.
63		userRepo := repository.NewUserRepository(db)
64		sessionRepo := repository.NewSessionRepository(db)
65		materialRepo := repository.NewMaterialRepository(db)
66		engagementRepo := repository.NewEngagementRepository(db)
67		orderRepo := repository.NewOrderRepository(db)
68		distributionRepo := repository.NewDistributionRepository(db)
69		messagingRepo := repository.NewMessagingRepository(db)
70		moderationRepo := repository.NewModerationRepository(db)
71		analyticsRepo := repository.NewAnalyticsRepository(db)
72		adminRepo := repository.NewAdminRepository(db)
73		courseRepo := repository.NewCourseRepository(db)
74	
75		// Wire config (minimal).
76		// EncryptionKey must be a valid 64-hex-char (32-byte) key so that
77		// encryptSensitiveField no longer falls back to plaintext storage.
78		cfg := &config.Config{
79			AppEnv:        "test",
80			SessionSecret: "test-secret",
81			EncryptionKey: "0102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f101112131415161718191a1b1c1d1e1f20",
82		}
83	
84		// Wire services.
85		authService := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, sessionRepo, cfg)
86		materialService := services.NewMaterialService(materialRepo, engagementRepo)
87		orderService := services.NewOrderService(orderRepo, materialRepo)
88		courseService := services.NewCourseService(courseRepo, materialRepo)
89		distributionService := services.NewDistributionService(distributionRepo, orderRepo, materialRepo)
90		messagingService := services.NewMessagingService(messagingRepo)
91		moderationService := services.NewModerationService(moderationRepo)
92		analyticsService := services.NewAnalyticsService(analyticsRepo)
93		adminService := services.NewAdminService(adminRepo, userRepo, materialRepo)
94	
95		// Wire handlers.
96		authHandler := handlers.NewAuthHandler(authService)
97		materialHandler := handlers.NewMaterialHandler(materialService)
98		orderHandler := handlers.NewOrderHandler(orderService)
99		courseHandler := handlers.NewCourseHandler(courseService)
100		distributionHandler := handlers.NewDistributionHandler(distributionService)
101		messagingHandler := handlers.NewMessagingHandler(messagingService)
102		moderationHandler := handlers.NewModerationHandler(moderationService, messagingService)
103		analyticsHandler := handlers.NewAnalyticsHandler(analyticsService)
104		adminHandler := handlers.NewAdminHandler(adminService, authService)
105	
106		// Wire middleware.
107		authMiddleware := middleware.NewAuthMiddleware(sessionRepo, userRepo, cfg.SessionSecret)
108		requireAuth := authMiddleware.RequireAuth()
109	
110		// Per-role RBAC middleware helpers.
111		requireClerkOrAdmin := middleware.RequireRole("clerk", "admin")
112		requireModOrAdmin := middleware.RequireRole("moderator", "admin")
113		requireInstrOrAdmin := middleware.RequireRole("instructor", "admin")
114		requireAdmin := middleware.RequireRole("admin")
115	
116		loginLimiter := middleware.NewRateLimiter(10, time.Minute)
117		loginRateLimit := loginLimiter.Middleware(func(c *fiber.Ctx) string { return c.IP() })
118		commentRateLimit := middleware.CommentRateLimit()
119	
120		// Template engine — point at the real templates directory relative to
121		// this package (internal/integration → ../../web/templates).
122		// Register custom template functions that are used in the templates.
123		engine := html.New("../../web/templates", ".html")
124		// Templates use custom math functions and type conversions.
125		engine.AddFunc("mul", func(a, b float64) float64 { return a * b })
126		engine.AddFunc("add", func(a, b int) int { return a + b })
127		engine.AddFunc("sub", func(a, b float64) float64 { return a - b })
128		engine.AddFunc("div", func(a, b float64) float64 {
129			if b == 0 {
130				return 0
131			}
132			return a / b
133		})
134		engine.AddFunc("float64", func(v interface{}) float64 {
135			switch n := v.(type) {
136			case int:
137				return float64(n)
138			case int64:
139				return float64(n)
140			case float64:
141				return n
142			}
143			return 0
144		})
145		engine.AddFunc("dict", func(values ...interface{}) (map[string]interface{}, error) {
146			if len(values)%2 != 0 {
147				return nil, fmt.Errorf("dict requires even number of arguments")
148			}
149			dict := make(map[string]interface{}, len(values)/2)
150			for i := 0; i < len(values); i += 2 {
151				key, ok := values[i].(string)
152				if !ok {
153					return nil, fmt.Errorf("dict keys must be strings")
154				}
155				dict[key] = values[i+1]
156			}
157			return dict, nil
158		})
159		engine.AddFunc("dec", func(n int) int { return n - 1 })
160		engine.AddFunc("inc", func(n int) int { return n + 1 })
161		engine.AddFunc("deref", func(v interface{}) interface{} {
162			if v == nil {
163				return nil
164			}
165			return v
166		})
167		engine.AddFunc("hourRange", func() []map[string]interface{} {
168			hours := make([]map[string]interface{}, 24)
169			for i := range hours {
170				hours[i] = map[string]interface{}{
171					"Val":   i,
172					"Label": fmt.Sprintf("%02d:00", i),
173				}
174			}
175			return hours
176		})
177	
178		app := fiber.New(fiber.Config{
179			Views:       engine,
180			ViewsLayout: "layouts/main",
181			// Don't return the default 500-page HTML; return plain text errors so
182			// test assertions on status codes are clean.
183			ErrorHandler: func(c *fiber.Ctx, err error) error {
184				code := fiber.StatusInternalServerError
185				if e, ok := err.(*fiber.Error); ok {
186					code = e.Code
187				}
188				return c.Status(code).SendString(err.Error())
189			},
190		})
191	
192		// Inject enc_key so admin handlers don't panic.
193		app.Use(func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
194			c.Locals("enc_key", cfg.EncryptionKey)
195			return c.Next()
196		})
197	
198		// Apply CSRF middleware globally, mirroring production.
199		// In production, CSRF runs after RequireAuth (it's added via group.Use after the
200		// group constructor sets RequireAuth). Replicate that ordering by skipping CSRF
201		// for unauthenticated requests — RequireAuth will reject those with 401/302.
202		app.Use(fibercsrf.New(fibercsrf.Config{
203			KeyLookup:      "header:X-Csrf-Token",
204			CookieName:     "csrf_token",
205			CookieHTTPOnly: false,
206			CookieSameSite: "Strict",
207			Extractor: func(c *fiber.Ctx) (string, error) {
208				if tok := c.Get("X-Csrf-Token"); tok != "" {
209					return tok, nil
210				}
211				if tok := c.FormValue("csrf_token"); tok != "" {
212					return tok, nil
213				}
214				return "", fmt.Errorf("CSRF token not found")
215			},
216			Next: func(c *fiber.Ctx) bool {
217				p := c.Path()
218				if p == "/login" || strings.HasPrefix(p, "/share/") || p == "/health" {
219					return true
220				}
221				// Skip for unauthenticated requests: auth middleware handles them.
222				return c.Cookies("session_token") == ""
223			},
224		}))
225	
226		// Health.
227		app.Get("/health", func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
228			return c.JSON(fiber.Map{"status": "ok"})
229		})
230	
231		// ------------------------------------------------------------------
232		// Public routes (no auth required)
233		// ------------------------------------------------------------------
234		app.Get("/login", authHandler.LoginPage)
235		app.Post("/login", loginRateLimit, authHandler.Login)
236		app.Get("/share/:token", materialHandler.SharedList)
237	
238		// ------------------------------------------------------------------
239		// Protected routes — RequireAuth only
240		// ------------------------------------------------------------------
241		app.Get("/account/change-password",  requireAuth, authHandler.ChangePasswordPage)
242		app.Post("/account/change-password", requireAuth, authHandler.ChangePassword)
243	
244		app.Post("/logout", requireAuth, authHandler.Logout)
245	
246		app.Get("/dashboard", requireAuth, func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
247			user := middleware.GetUser(c)
248			switch user.Role {
249			case "admin":
250				return c.Redirect("/dashboard/admin", fiber.StatusFound)
251			case "instructor":
252				return c.Redirect("/dashboard/instructor", fiber.StatusFound)
253			default:
254				return c.Render("dashboard", fiber.Map{
255					"Title": "Dashboard",
256					"User":  user,
257				}, "layouts/base")
258			}
259		})
260	
261		// Browse history
262		app.Get("/history", requireAuth, materialHandler.BrowseHistory)
263	
264		// Materials catalog
265		app.Get("/materials", requireAuth, materialHandler.ListPage)
266		app.Get("/materials/search", requireAuth, materialHandler.SearchPartial)
267		app.Get("/materials/:id", requireAuth, materialHandler.DetailPage)
268	
269		app.Post("/materials/:id/rate", requireAuth, materialHandler.Rate)
270		app.Post("/materials/:id/comments", requireAuth, commentRateLimit, materialHandler.AddComment)
271		app.Post("/comments/:id/report", requireAuth, materialHandler.ReportComment)
272	
273		// Favorites
274		app.Get("/favorites", requireAuth, materialHandler.FavoritesList)
275		app.Post("/favorites", requireAuth, materialHandler.CreateFavoritesList)
276		app.Get("/favorites/:id", requireAuth, materialHandler.GetFavoritesListDetail)
277		app.Get("/favorites/:id/items", requireAuth, materialHandler.GetFavoritesListItems)
278		app.Post("/favorites/:id/items", requireAuth, materialHandler.AddToFavorites)
279		app.Delete("/favorites/:id/items/:materialID", requireAuth, materialHandler.RemoveFromFavorites)
280		app.Get("/favorites/:id/share", requireAuth, materialHandler.ShareFavoritesList)
281	
282		// Orders (student)
283		app.Get("/orders", requireAuth, orderHandler.ListOrders)
284		app.Get("/orders/cart", requireAuth, orderHandler.CartPage)
285		app.Get("/orders/:id", requireAuth, orderHandler.OrderDetail)
286	
287		app.Post("/orders", requireAuth, orderHandler.PlaceOrder)
288		app.Post("/orders/:id/pay", requireAuth, orderHandler.ConfirmPayment)
289		app.Post("/orders/:id/cancel", requireAuth, orderHandler.CancelOrder)
290		app.Post("/orders/:id/returns", requireAuth, orderHandler.SubmitReturnRequest)
291	
292		// Returns (student view)
293		app.Get("/returns", requireAuth, orderHandler.ListReturnRequests)
294	
295		// Inbox / messaging
296		app.Get("/inbox", requireAuth, messagingHandler.Inbox)
297		app.Get("/inbox/items", requireAuth, messagingHandler.InboxItems)
298		app.Post("/inbox/:id/read", requireAuth, messagingHandler.MarkRead)
299		app.Post("/inbox/read-all", requireAuth, messagingHandler.MarkAllRead)

393	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
394	// loginAs — creates a user with the given role, logs in, returns cookie string.
395	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
396	
397	// loginAs creates a user with the given role, sends POST /login, and returns the
398	// "session_token=<value>" cookie string suitable for use in subsequent requests.
399	// The returned string can be passed directly as the Cookie header value.
400	func loginAs(t *testing.T, app *fiber.App, db *sql.DB, role string) string {
401		t.Helper()
402	
403		// Create a unique username per call so parallel tests don't collide.
404		username := fmt.Sprintf("testuser_%s_%d", role, time.Now().UnixNano())
405		password := "TestPassword123!"
406		email := username + "@example.com"
407	
408		userRepo := repository.NewUserRepository(db)
409		sessionRepo := repository.NewSessionRepository(db)
410		cfg := &config.Config{SessionSecret: "test-secret"}
411		authSvc := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, sessionRepo, cfg)
412	
413		if _, err := authSvc.Register(username, email, password, role); err != nil {
414			t.Fatalf("loginAs: register user: %v", err)
415		}
416	
417		body := fmt.Sprintf("username=%s&password=%s", username, password)
418		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/login", strings.NewReader(body))
419		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
420	
421		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
422		if err != nil {
423			t.Fatalf("loginAs: app.Test: %v", err)
424		}
425		defer resp.Body.Close()
426	
427		// Login success redirects to /dashboard (302).
428		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
429			b, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
430			t.Fatalf("loginAs: unexpected status %d; body: %s", resp.StatusCode, string(b))
431		}
432	
433		// Extract the Set-Cookie header.
434		for _, c := range resp.Cookies() {
435			if c.Name == "session_token" {
436				return "session_token=" + c.Value
437			}
438		}
439		t.Fatal("loginAs: no session_token cookie found in response")
440		return ""
441	}
442	
443	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
444	// makeRequest — fires a request against the test Fiber app.
445	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
446	
447	// makeRequest builds a request and calls app.Test.
448	// - method: HTTP verb ("GET", "POST", etc.)
449	// - path: request path ("/orders/1")
450	// - body: raw request body (empty string for no body)
451	// - cookie: cookie header value (e.g. "session_token=abc123"); empty for none
452	// - contentType: e.g. "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"; empty defaults to no Content-Type
453	func makeRequest(app *fiber.App, method, path, body, cookie, contentType string, extraHeaders ...map[string]string) *http.Response {
454		var bodyReader io.Reader
455		if body != "" {
456			bodyReader = strings.NewReader(body)
457		}
458		req := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, bodyReader)
459		if cookie != "" {
460			req.Header.Set("Cookie", cookie)
461		}
462		if contentType != "" {
463			req.Header.Set("Content-Type", contentType)
464		}
465		for _, h := range extraHeaders {
466			for k, v := range h {
467				req.Header.Set(k, v)
468			}
469		}
470		// For state-changing methods with an authenticated session, automatically
471		// fetch and attach the CSRF token unless the caller already provided one.
472		if cookie != "" && req.Header.Get("X-Csrf-Token") == "" {
473			m := strings.ToUpper(method)
474			if m == http.MethodPost || m == http.MethodPut || m == http.MethodDelete || m == http.MethodPatch {
475				if tok := fetchCSRFToken(app, cookie); tok != "" {
476					req.Header.Set("X-Csrf-Token", tok)
477					req.Header.Set("Cookie", cookie+"; csrf_token="+tok)
478				}
479			}
480		}
481		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
482		if err != nil {
483			// Return a synthetic 500 response on internal test errors.
484			return &http.Response{StatusCode: http.StatusInternalServerError}
485		}
486		return resp
487	}
488	
489	// fetchCSRFToken performs a GET to a CSRF-protected route to obtain the
490	// csrf_token cookie value. The cookie value IS the token (double-submit pattern).
491	// Returns empty string if the request fails or no csrf_token cookie is present.
492	func fetchCSRFToken(app *fiber.App, sessionCookie string) string {
493		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/materials", nil)
494		if sessionCookie != "" {
495			req.Header.Set("Cookie", sessionCookie)
496		}
497		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
498		if err != nil {
499			return ""
500		}
501		defer resp.Body.Close()
502		for _, c := range resp.Cookies() {
503			if c.Name == "csrf_token" {
504				return c.Value
505			}
506		}
507		return ""
508	}
509	
510	// htmxHeaders returns a map with HX-Request: true for use with makeRequest's
511	// extraHeaders parameter.
512	func htmxHeaders() map[string]string {
513		return map[string]string{"HX-Request": "true"}
514	}

606	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
607	// readBody — helper to read response body as string.
608	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
609	
610	func readBody(resp *http.Response) string {
611		if resp == nil || resp.Body == nil {
612			return ""
613		}
614		defer resp.Body.Close()
615		b, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
616		return string(b)
617	}

1	package crypto
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/aes"
5		"crypto/cipher"
6		"crypto/hmac"
7		"crypto/rand"
8		"crypto/sha256"
9		"encoding/base64"
10		"encoding/hex"
11		"errors"
12		"fmt"
13		"io"
14		"strings"
15		"unicode/utf8"
16	
17		"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
18	)
19	
20	// blindIndexSalt domain-separates the HMAC sub-key from the AES encryption
21	// key so that blind-index tokens are independent from ciphertext operations.
22	const blindIndexSalt = "blind_index_v1"
23	
24	// BlindIndex returns a deterministic hex-encoded HMAC-SHA256 of value keyed
25	// by a sub-key derived from encKey.  The result allows SQL equality matching
26	// on sensitive fields while the primary storage remains randomized AES-GCM.
27	//
28	// encKey must be exactly 32 bytes (the AES-256 key used for EncryptField).
29	// Returns an empty string if encKey is not exactly 32 bytes.
30	func BlindIndex(encKey []byte, value string) string {
31		if len(encKey) != 32 {
32			return ""
33		}
34		// Derive a domain-specific sub-key.
35		prk := hmac.New(sha256.New, encKey)
36		prk.Write([]byte(blindIndexSalt))
37		indexKey := prk.Sum(nil)
38	
39		mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, indexKey)
40		mac.Write([]byte(value))
41		return hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
42	}
43	
44	// EncryptField encrypts plaintext using AES-256-GCM with the supplied 32-byte
45	// key.  The nonce is prepended to the ciphertext and the whole thing is
46	// returned as standard base64 so it is safe to store in a TEXT column.
47	func EncryptField(key []byte, plaintext string) (string, error) {
48		if len(key) != 32 {
49			return "", fmt.Errorf("crypto: encryption key must be 32 bytes, got %d", len(key))
50		}
51	
52		block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
53		if err != nil {
54			return "", fmt.Errorf("crypto: create cipher: %w", err)
55		}
56	
57		gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
58		if err != nil {
59			return "", fmt.Errorf("crypto: create GCM: %w", err)
60		}
61	
62		nonce := make([]byte, gcm.NonceSize())
63		if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, nonce); err != nil {
64			return "", fmt.Errorf("crypto: generate nonce: %w", err)
65		}
66	
67		// Seal appends the ciphertext+tag to nonce.
68		ciphertext := gcm.Seal(nonce, nonce, []byte(plaintext), nil)
69		return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(ciphertext), nil
70	}
71	
72	// DecryptField is the inverse of EncryptField.  It decodes the base64 blob,
73	// extracts the nonce, and returns the plaintext.
74	func DecryptField(key []byte, ciphertext string) (string, error) {
75		if len(key) != 32 {
76			return "", fmt.Errorf("crypto: encryption key must be 32 bytes, got %d", len(key))
77		}
78	
79		data, err := base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(ciphertext)
80		if err != nil {
81			return "", fmt.Errorf("crypto: base64 decode: %w", err)
82		}
83	
84		block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
85		if err != nil {
86			return "", fmt.Errorf("crypto: create cipher: %w", err)
87		}
88	
89		gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
90		if err != nil {
91			return "", fmt.Errorf("crypto: create GCM: %w", err)
92		}
93	
94		nonceSize := gcm.NonceSize()
95		if len(data) < nonceSize {
96			return "", errors.New("crypto: ciphertext too short")
97		}
98	
99		nonce, data := data[:nonceSize], data[nonceSize:]
100		plaintext, err := gcm.Open(nil, nonce, data, nil)
101		if err != nil {
102			return "", fmt.Errorf("crypto: decrypt: %w", err)
103		}
104	
105		return string(plaintext), nil
106	}
107	
108	// HashPassword hashes password using bcrypt at cost 12.
109	func HashPassword(password string) (string, error) {
110		hash, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(password), 12)
111		if err != nil {
112			return "", fmt.Errorf("crypto: hash password: %w", err)
113		}
114		return string(hash), nil
115	}
116	
117	// CheckPassword returns true when password matches the stored bcrypt hash.
118	func CheckPassword(hash, password string) bool {
119		return bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(hash), []byte(password)) == nil
120	}
121	
122	// MaskName converts a full name into initials with a trailing period on each
123	// part.  Examples:
124	//
125	//	"John Doe"       → "J. D."
126	//	"Alice"          → "A."
127	//	"Mary Jane Watson" → "M. J. W."
128	//
129	// Empty input is returned unchanged.
130	func MaskName(name string) string {
131		name = strings.TrimSpace(name)
132		if name == "" {
133			return ""
134		}
135	
136		parts := strings.Fields(name)
137		var sb strings.Builder
138		for i, part := range parts {
139			r, size := utf8.DecodeRuneInString(part)
140			if size == 0 {
141				continue
142			}
143			_ = r
144			// Write just the first Unicode character of each word followed by ".".
145			sb.WriteString(string([]rune(part)[:1]))
146			sb.WriteByte('.')
147			if i < len(parts)-1 {
148				sb.WriteByte(' ')
149			}
150		}
151		return sb.String()
152	}
153	
154	// MaskID masks all but the last four characters of id with asterisks.
155	// Examples:
156	//
157	//	"1234567890"  → "******7890"
158	//	"AB12"        → "AB12"   (≤4 chars: returned as-is)
159	//	""            → ""
160	func MaskID(id string) string {
161		runes := []rune(id)
162		n := len(runes)
163		if n <= 4 {
164			return id
165		}
166		masked := make([]rune, n)
167		for i := 0; i < n-4; i++ {
168			masked[i] = '*'
169		}
170		copy(masked[n-4:], runes[n-4:])
171		return string(masked)
172	}

1	package handlers

13	// AnalyticsHandler handles all analytics, KPI, geospatial, and export routes.
14	type AnalyticsHandler struct {
15		analyticsService *services.AnalyticsService
16	}
17	
18	// NewAnalyticsHandler creates an AnalyticsHandler backed by the given service.
19	func NewAnalyticsHandler(as *services.AnalyticsService) *AnalyticsHandler {
20		return &AnalyticsHandler{analyticsService: as}
21	}
22	
23	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
24	// Dashboard routes
25	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
26	
27	// AdminDashboard handles GET /dashboard/admin
28	// Renders the admin analytics dashboard with full KPI data.
29	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) AdminDashboard(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
30		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
31	
32		data, err := h.analyticsService.AdminDashboardData()
33		if err != nil {
34			return internalErr(c, observability.App, "load admin dashboard data failed", err, "user_id", user.ID)
35		}
36	
37		// Compute total and pending counts for the KPI cards.
38		total := 0
39		for _, n := range data.OrdersByStatus {
40			total += n
41		}
42		pending := data.OrdersByStatus["pending_payment"] + data.OrdersByStatus["pending_shipment"]
43	
44		return c.Render("analytics/admin_dashboard", fiber.Map{
45			"Title":           "Admin Dashboard",
46			"User":            user,
47			"ActivePage":      "analytics",
48			"Dashboard":       data,
49			"TotalOrders":     total,
50			"PendingOrders":   pending,
51			"FulfillmentRate": fmt.Sprintf("%.1f", data.FulfillmentRate),
52			"ReturnRate":      fmt.Sprintf("%.1f", data.ReturnRate),
53			"ActiveUsers":     data.ActiveUsers,
54			"Inventory":       data.Inventory,
55			"TopMaterials":    data.TopMaterials,
56			"OrdersByStatus":  data.OrdersByStatus,
57			"GMV":             fmt.Sprintf("%.2f", data.GMV),
58			"AOV":             fmt.Sprintf("%.2f", data.AOV),

-- Adds an R*Tree virtual table on the locations table to support fast
-- bounding-box pre-filtering before Point-in-Polygon containment tests.
--
-- Coordinate convention:
--   minX / maxX  →  longitude range
--   minY / maxY  →  latitude range
--
-- For point locations (lat/lng present), minX=maxX=lng and minY=maxY=lat.
-- For polygon regions, the application populates the bounding box from the
-- WKT geometry when calling ComputeRegionAggregation.

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS location_rtree USING rtree(
    id,
    minX, maxX,
    minY, maxY
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------
-- Triggers: keep location_rtree in sync with locations table.
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS location_rtree_ai
AFTER INSERT ON locations
WHEN new.lat IS NOT NULL AND new.lng IS NOT NULL
BEGIN
    INSERT OR IGNORE INTO location_rtree (id, minX, maxX, minY, maxY)
    VALUES (new.id, new.lng, new.lng, new.lat, new.lat);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS location_rtree_au
AFTER UPDATE OF lat, lng ON locations
BEGIN
    DELETE FROM location_rtree WHERE id = old.id;
    INSERT OR IGNORE INTO location_rtree (id, minX, maxX, minY, maxY)
    SELECT new.id, new.lng, new.lng, new.lat, new.lat
    WHERE  new.lat IS NOT NULL AND new.lng IS NOT NULL;
END;

CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS location_rtree_ad
AFTER DELETE ON locations
BEGIN
    DELETE FROM location_rtree WHERE id = old.id;
END;
"}

-- Immutable audit trail for every mutation on entity_custom_fields.
-- Captures who changed what, when, and why for every set/delete operation.
-- Rows in this table are never updated or deleted.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS entity_custom_fields_audit (
    id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    entity_type  TEXT    NOT NULL,
    entity_id    INTEGER NOT NULL,
    field_name   TEXT    NOT NULL,
    old_value    TEXT,           -- NULL when the field did not previously exist
    new_value    TEXT,           -- NULL when the field is deleted
    is_encrypted INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    actor_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    reason       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    changed_at   TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ecf_audit_entity
ON entity_custom_fields_audit(entity_type, entity_id);
"}

451	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
452	// Extended spatial analysis
453	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
454	
455	// haversineKm returns the great-circle distance in kilometres between two
456	// lat/lng points using the Haversine formula.
457	func haversineKm(lat1, lng1, lat2, lng2 float64) float64 {
458		const earthRadiusKm = 6371.0
459		dLat := (lat2 - lat1) * math.Pi / 180.0
460		dLng := (lng2 - lng1) * math.Pi / 180.0
461		a := math.Sin(dLat/2)*math.Sin(dLat/2) +
462			math.Cos(lat1*math.Pi/180.0)*math.Cos(lat2*math.Pi/180.0)*
463				math.Sin(dLng/2)*math.Sin(dLng/2)
464		return earthRadiusKm * 2 * math.Atan2(math.Sqrt(a), math.Sqrt(1-a))
465	}
466	
467	// LocationsWithinRadius returns all locations (optionally filtered by locType)
468	// whose geodesic distance from (centerLat, centerLng) is ≤ radiusKm.
469	// Uses the Haversine formula computed in Go (no spatial extension required).
470	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) LocationsWithinRadius(centerLat, centerLng, radiusKm float64, locType string) ([]models.Location, error) {
471		all, err := r.GetLocations(locType)
472		if err != nil {
473			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: LocationsWithinRadius: %w", err)
474		}
475	
476		var out []models.Location
477		for _, loc := range all {
478			if loc.Lat == nil || loc.Lng == nil {
479				continue
480			}
481			if haversineKm(centerLat, centerLng, *loc.Lat, *loc.Lng) <= radiusKm {
482				out = append(out, loc)
483			}
484		}
485		return out, nil
486	}
487	
488	// POIDensityWithinRadius counts the number of locations of each type within
489	// radiusKm of (centerLat, centerLng).  Returns a map of locationType → count.
490	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) POIDensityWithinRadius(centerLat, centerLng, radiusKm float64) (map[string]int, error) {
491		locs, err := r.LocationsWithinRadius(centerLat, centerLng, radiusKm, "")
492		if err != nil {
493			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: POIDensityWithinRadius: %w", err)
494		}
495	
496		density := make(map[string]int)
497		for _, loc := range locs {
498			t := "unknown"
499			if loc.Type != nil {
500				t = *loc.Type
501			}
502			density[t]++
503		}
504		return density, nil
505	}

509	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) TrajectoryPoints(materialID int64) ([]models.TrajectoryPoint, error) {
510		const q = `
511			SELECT
512				COALESCE(scan_id, ''),
513				event_type,
514				COALESCE(custody_from, ''),
515				COALESCE(custody_to,   ''),

517			FROM   distribution_events

520	
521		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, materialID)

524		}
525		defer rows.Close()
526	
527		var out []models.TrajectoryPoint

530			if err := rows.Scan(&tp.ScanID, &tp.EventType, &tp.CustodyFrom, &tp.CustodyTo, &tp.OccurredAt); err != nil {
531				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: TrajectoryPoints scan: %w", err)
532			}
533			out = append(out, tp)
534		}
535		return out, rows.Err()

538	// regionEventPoint is an internal type used during region-stats computation.
539	type regionEventPoint struct {
540		id          int64
541		orderID     int64
542		custodyFrom string
543		custodyTo   string
544	}
545	
546	// RegionStats aggregates order and distribution-scan counts by admin-region
547	// using Haversine-based spatial containment.  Distribution events are spatially
548	// assigned to a region when their custody_from or custody_to string matches the
549	// name of a non-region location that falls within 50 km of the region centroid.
550	// This produces deterministic, correct per-region counts without requiring
551	// PostGIS or a dedicated region_id FK on the orders/events tables.
552	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) RegionStats() ([]models.RegionStat, error) {
553		// 1. Load all admin-region centroids.
554		regions, err := r.GetLocations("admin_region")
555		if err != nil {
556			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: RegionStats: fetch regions: %w", err)
557		}
558	
559		// 2. Load all non-admin-region locations with coordinates (name → lat/lng map).
560		type locPt struct{ lat, lng float64 }
561		const locQ = `SELECT name, lat, lng FROM locations WHERE type != 'admin_region' AND lat IS NOT NULL AND lng IS NOT NULL`
562		locRows, err := r.db.Query(locQ)
563		if err != nil {
564			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: RegionStats: fetch locations: %w", err)
565		}
566		defer locRows.Close()
567		locByName := make(map[string]locPt)
568		for locRows.Next() {
569			var name string
570			var pt locPt
571			if err := locRows.Scan(&name, &pt.lat, &pt.lng); err != nil {
572				continue
573			}
574			locByName[name] = pt
575		}
576		locRows.Close()
577	
578		// 3. Load all distribution events with their custody annotations.
579		const deQ = `SELECT id, COALESCE(order_id, 0), COALESCE(custody_from, ''), COALESCE(custody_to, '') FROM distribution_events`
580		deRows, err := r.db.Query(deQ)
581		if err != nil {
582			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: RegionStats: fetch events: %w", err)
583		}
584		defer deRows.Close()
585		var events []regionEventPoint
586		for deRows.Next() {
587			var ev regionEventPoint
588			if err := deRows.Scan(&ev.id, &ev.orderID, &ev.custodyFrom, &ev.custodyTo); err != nil {
589				continue
590			}
591			events = append(events, ev)
592		}
593		deRows.Close()
594	
595		const radiusKm = 50.0
596	
597		// 4. For each region, collect the set of nearby location names (Haversine),
598		//    then count events and distinct orders that reference those locations.
599		out := make([]models.RegionStat, 0, len(regions))
600		for _, reg := range regions {
601			if reg.Lat == nil || reg.Lng == nil {
602				continue
603			}
604			rLat, rLng := *reg.Lat, *reg.Lng
605	
606			nearbyNames := make(map[string]bool)
607			for name, pt := range locByName {
608				if haversineKm(rLat, rLng, pt.lat, pt.lng) <= radiusKm {
609					nearbyNames[name] = true
610				}
611			}
612	
613			orderSet := make(map[int64]bool)
614			var scanCount int
615			for _, ev := range events {
616				if nearbyNames[ev.custodyFrom] || nearbyNames[ev.custodyTo] {
617					scanCount++
618					if ev.orderID > 0 {
619						orderSet[ev.orderID] = true
620					}
621				}
622			}
623	
624			out = append(out, models.RegionStat{
625				RegionName: reg.Name,
626				OrderCount: len(orderSet),
627				ScanCount:  scanCount,
628				Lat:        rLat,
629				Lng:        rLng,
630			})
631		}
632	
633		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].RegionName < out[j].RegionName })
634		return out, nil

940	type FunnelStage struct {
941		Stage string
942		Count int
943	}

945	// OrderFunnel returns the drop-off counts at each order pipeline stage in the
946	// canonical order: pending_payment → pending_shipment → in_transit → completed.
947	// The canceled stage is also included for reference.
948	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) OrderFunnel() ([]FunnelStage, error) {
949		const q = `
950			SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS cnt
951			FROM   orders
952			WHERE  status IN ('pending_payment', 'pending_shipment', 'in_transit', 'completed', 'canceled')
953			GROUP  BY status`
954	
955		rows, err := r.db.Query(q)
956		if err != nil {
957			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: OrderFunnel: %w", err)
958		}
959		defer rows.Close()
960	
961		// Build a map first, then return in canonical stage order.
962		counts := make(map[string]int)
963		for rows.Next() {
964			var status string
965			var cnt int
966			if err := rows.Scan(&status, &cnt); err != nil {
967				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: OrderFunnel scan: %w", err)
968			}
969			counts[status] = cnt
970		}
971		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
972			return nil, err
973		}
974	
975		order := []string{"pending_payment", "pending_shipment", "in_transit", "completed", "canceled"}
976		out := make([]FunnelStage, 0, len(order))
977		for _, s := range order {
978			out = append(out, FunnelStage{Stage: s, Count: counts[s]})
979		}
980		return out, nil
981	}

   - HMAC blind indexes (deterministic) for SQL equality matching on encrypted fields
