324	// GetListByShareToken returns the favorites list matching the given share token.
325	// Returns ErrShareExpired if the token exists but has expired, sql.ErrNoRows if
326	// no such token exists at all.
327	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetListByShareToken(token string) (*models.FavoritesList, error) {
328		const q = `
329			SELECT id, user_id, name, visibility, share_token, share_expires_at, created_at
330			FROM   favorites_lists
331			WHERE  share_token = ?`
332	
333		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, token)
334		fl := &models.FavoritesList{}
335		err := row.Scan(&fl.ID, &fl.UserID, &fl.Name, &fl.Visibility,
336			&fl.ShareToken, &fl.ShareExpiresAt, &fl.CreatedAt)
337		if err != nil {
338			return nil, err
339		}
340		// Check expiry in Go so callers can distinguish expired vs not-found.
341		if fl.ShareExpiresAt != nil {
342			if exp, parseErr := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, *fl.ShareExpiresAt); parseErr == nil {
343				if time.Now().After(exp) {
344					return nil, ErrShareExpired
345				}
346			}
347		}
348		return fl, nil
349	}

392	// GenerateShareToken creates a crypto-random share token, stores it on the
393	// favorites list, and returns the raw token string.
394	func (r *EngagementRepository) GenerateShareToken(listID int64, expiresAt time.Time) (string, error) {
395		b := make([]byte, 24)
396		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
397			return "", fmt.Errorf("repository: GenerateShareToken: rand: %w", err)
398		}
399		token := hex.EncodeToString(b)
400	
401		const q = `
402			UPDATE favorites_lists
403			SET    share_token = ?, share_expires_at = ?
404			WHERE  id = ?`
405		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, token, expiresAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), listID)
406		if err != nil {
407			return "", err
408		}
409		return token, nil
410	}

415		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, visibility, listID)
416		return err
417	}

21	
22	// EngagementRepository provides database operations for browse history, ratings,
23	// comments, comment reports, and favorites lists/items.
24	type EngagementRepository struct {
25		db *sql.DB
26	}
27	
28	// NewEngagementRepository returns an EngagementRepository backed by the given database.
29	func NewEngagementRepository(db *sql.DB) *EngagementRepository {
30		return &EngagementRepository{db: db}
31	}
32	
33	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
34	// Browse history
35	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
36	
37	// RecordVisit inserts (or replaces) a browse history row for the given user + material.
38	func (r *EngagementRepository) RecordVisit(userID, materialID int64) error {
39		const q = `
40			INSERT INTO browse_history (user_id, material_id, visited_at)
41			VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))`
42		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, userID, materialID)
43		return err
44	}
45	
46	// GetHistory returns the most-recently visited materials for a user.
47	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetHistory(userID int64, limit int) ([]models.BrowseHistory, error) {
48		const q = `
49			SELECT id, user_id, material_id, visited_at
50			FROM   browse_history
51			WHERE  user_id = ?
52			ORDER  BY visited_at DESC
53			LIMIT  ?`
54	
55		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, userID, limit)
56		if err != nil {
57		return nil, err
58		}
59		defer rows.Close()
60	
61		var items []models.BrowseHistory
62		for rows.Next() {
63			var bh models.BrowseHistory
64			if err := rows.Scan(&bh.ID, &bh.UserID, &bh.MaterialID, &bh.VisitedAt); err != nil {
65				return nil, err
66			}
67			items = append(items, bh)
68		}
69		return items, rows.Err()
70	}
71	
72	// GetHistoryItems returns browse history joined with material titles for a user.
73	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetHistoryItems(userID int64, limit int) ([]models.HistoryItem, error) {
74		const q = `
75			SELECT bh.material_id, m.title, bh.visited_at
76			FROM   browse_history bh
77			JOIN   materials m ON m.id = bh.material_id
78			WHERE  bh.user_id = ?
79			ORDER  BY bh.visited_at DESC
80			LIMIT  ?`
81	
82		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, userID, limit)
83		if err != nil {
84			return nil, err
85		}
86		defer rows.Close()
87	
88		var items []models.HistoryItem
89		for rows.Next() {
90			var hi models.HistoryItem
91			if err := rows.Scan(&hi.MaterialID, &hi.MaterialTitle, &hi.VisitedAt); err != nil {
92				return nil, err
93			}
94			items = append(items, hi)
95		}
96		return items, rows.Err()
97	}
98

100	// Ratings
101	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
102	
103	// InsertRating records a star rating for a material/user pair exactly once.
104	// Returns ErrAlreadyRated if the user has already rated this material.
105	func (r *EngagementRepository) InsertRating(materialID, userID int64, stars int) error {
106		const q = `
107			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ratings (material_id, user_id, stars, created_at)
108			VALUES (?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`
109		res, err := r.db.Exec(q, materialID, userID, stars)
110		if err != nil {
111			return err
112		}
113		n, _ := res.RowsAffected()
114		if n == 0 {
115			return ErrAlreadyRated
116		}
117		return nil
118	}
119	
120	// GetRating returns the rating a specific user gave to a material, or nil if none.
121	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetRating(materialID, userID int64) (*models.Rating, error) {
122		const q = `
123			SELECT id, material_id, user_id, stars, created_at

208	// ReportComment records a report for a comment.
209	// After inserting the report, it counts unique reporters; if >= 3, the comment
210	// status is set to 'collapsed'.
211	func (r *EngagementRepository) ReportComment(commentID, reportedBy int64, reason string) error {
212		tx, err := r.db.Begin()
212		if err != nil {
213			return err
214		}
215		defer func() {
216			if err != nil {
217				_ = tx.Rollback()
218			}
219		}()
220	
221		const insertReport = `
222			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO comment_reports (comment_id, reported_by, reason, created_at)
223			VALUES (?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`
224		_, err = tx.Exec(insertReport, commentID, reportedBy, reason)
225		if err != nil {
226			return err
227		}
228	
229		var uniqueReporters int
230		const countQ = `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT reported_by) FROM comment_reports WHERE comment_id = ?`
231		err = tx.QueryRow(countQ, commentID).Scan(&uniqueReporters)
232		if err != nil {
233			return err
234		}
235	
236		if uniqueReporters >= 3 {
237		const collapseQ = `UPDATE comments SET status = 'collapsed', updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND status = 'visible'`
238			if _, err = tx.Exec(collapseQ, commentID); err != nil {
239				return err
240			}
241		}
242	
243		return tx.Commit()
244	}

246	// CountRecentComments returns the number of comments a user has posted since the given time.
247	// Uses UNIX epoch integers for comparison to avoid SQLite space-separator vs RFC3339 T-separator
248	// format mismatch that would make the rate-limit check always pass.
249	func (r *EngagementRepository) CountRecentComments(userID int64, since time.Time) (int, error) {
250		const q = `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM comments WHERE user_id = ?
251			AND CAST(strftime('%s', created_at) AS INTEGER) >= ?`
252		var count int
253		err := r.db.QueryRow(q, userID, since.UTC().Unix()).Scan(&count)
254		return count, err
255	}

318	// GetListByShareToken returns the favorites list matching the given share token.
319	// Returns ErrShareExpired if the token exists but has expired, sql.ErrNoRows if
320	// no such token exists at all.
321	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetListByShareToken(token string) (*models.FavoritesList, error) {
321		const q = `
322			SELECT id, user_id, name, visibility, share_token, share_expires_at, created_at
323			FROM   favorites_lists
324			WHERE  share_token = ?`
325	
326		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, token)
327		fl := &models.FavoritesList{}
328		err := row.Scan(&fl.ID, &fl.UserID, &fl.Name, &fl.Visibility,
329			&fl.ShareToken, &fl.ShareExpiresAt, &fl.CreatedAt)
330		if err != nil {
331			return nil, err
332		}
333		// Check expiry in Go so callers can distinguish expired vs not-found.
334		if fl.ShareExpiresAt != nil {
335			if exp, parseErr := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, *fl.ShareExpiresAt); parseErr == nil {
336				if time.Now().After(exp) {
337					return nil, ErrShareExpired
338				}
339			}
340		}
341		return fl, nil
342	}

382	// GenerateShareToken creates a crypto-random share token, stores it on the
383	// favorites list, and returns the raw token string.
384	func (r *EngagementRepository) GenerateShareToken(listID int64, expiresAt time.Time) (string, error) {
384		b := make([]byte, 24)
385		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
386			return "", fmt.Errorf("repository: GenerateShareToken: rand: %w", err)
387		}
388		token := hex.EncodeToString(b)
389	
390		const q = `
390		UPDATE favorites_lists
391			SET    share_token = ?, share_expires_at = ?
392			WHERE  id = ?`
393		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, token, expiresAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), listID)
394		if err != nil {
395			return "", err
396		}
397		return token, nil
398	}

35	// GetPendingReview returns paginated comments whose status is 'collapsed',
36	// enriched with report counts, reporter usernames, material title, and author name.
37	func (r *ModerationRepository) GetPendingReview(limit, offset int) ([]ModerationItem, error) {
38		// Fetch collapsed comments with their material title and author name.
39		const q = `
39		SELECT c.id, c.material_id, c.user_id, c.body, c.link_count,
40			       c.status, c.report_count, c.created_at, c.updated_at,
41			       m.title      AS material_title,
42			       u.username   AS author_name
43			FROM   comments c
44			JOIN   materials m ON m.id = c.material_id
45			JOIN   users     u ON u.id = c.user_id
46			WHERE  c.status = 'collapsed'
47			ORDER  BY c.report_count DESC, c.created_at ASC
48			LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
49	
50		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, limit, offset)
51		if err != nil {
52			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: %w", err)
53		}
54		defer rows.Close()
55	
56		type rawRow struct {
56		comment       models.Comment
57			materialTitle string
58			authorName    string
59		}
60		var raws []rawRow
61	
62		for rows.Next() {
63			var rw rawRow
63			c := &rw.comment
64			if err := rows.Scan(
65				&c.ID, &c.MaterialID, &c.UserID, &c.Body, &c.LinkCount,
66				&c.Status, &c.ReportCount, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt,
67				&rw.materialTitle, &rw.authorName,
68			); err != nil {
69				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: scan: %w", err)
70			}
71			raws = append(raws, rw)
72		}
73		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
74			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: rows: %w", err)
75		}
76	
77		// For each comment, fetch the reporter usernames.
77		const reportersQ = `
78			SELECT u.username
79			FROM   comment_reports cr
80			JOIN   users u ON u.id = cr.reported_by
81			WHERE  cr.comment_id = ?
82			ORDER  BY cr.created_at ASC`
83	
84		items := make([]ModerationItem, 0, len(raws))
85		for _, rw := range raws {
85		rptRows, err := r.db.Query(reportersQ, rw.comment.ID)
86			if err != nil {
87				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: reporters query: %w", err)
88			}
89			var reporters []string
90			for rptRows.Next() {
90			var uname string
91				if err := rptRows.Scan(&uname); err != nil {
92					rptRows.Close()
93					return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: reporters scan: %w", err)
94				}
95				reporters = append(reporters, uname)
96			}
97			rptRows.Close()
97			if err := rptRows.Err(); err != nil {
98				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: reporters rows: %w", err)
99			}
100	
101			items = append(items, ModerationItem{
101			Comment:       rw.comment,
102				ReportCount:   rw.comment.ReportCount,
103				Reporters:     reporters,
104				MaterialTitle: rw.materialTitle,
105				AuthorName:    rw.authorName,
106			})
107		}
108		return items, nil
109	}

115			WHERE  id = ? AND status = 'collapsed'`

349	// ReissueItem handles lost or damaged copy replacement:
350	//   - Records a "lost" or "damaged" event for oldScanID (marking it out of
351	//     circulation).
352	//   - Records a new "issued" event for newScanID.
353	//
354	// reason must be "lost" or "damaged".
355	func (s *DistributionService) ReissueItem(orderID, materialID, actorID int64, oldScanID, newScanID, reason string) error {
356		if reason != "lost" && reason != "damaged" {
357			return fmt.Errorf("service: ReissueItem: invalid reason %q (must be 'lost' or 'damaged')", reason)
358		}
359		if oldScanID == "" || newScanID == "" {
360			return errors.New("service: ReissueItem: both oldScanID and newScanID are required")
361		}
362	
363		// Verify order exists.
364		if _, err := s.orderRepo.GetByID(orderID); err != nil {
365			if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
366				return fmt.Errorf("service: ReissueItem: order %d not found", orderID)
367			}
368			return fmt.Errorf("service: ReissueItem: load order: %w", err)
369		}
370	
371		orderIDPtr := &orderID
372		actorIDPtr := &actorID
373	
374		// Record the loss/damage event for the old scan ID.
375		oldEvt := &models.DistributionEvent{
376			OrderID:    orderIDPtr,
377			MaterialID: materialID,
377			Qty:        1,
378			EventType:  reason, // "lost" or "damaged"
379			ScanID:     &oldScanID,
380			ActorID:    actorIDPtr,
381		}
382		if _, err := s.distRepo.RecordEvent(oldEvt); err != nil {
383			return fmt.Errorf("service: ReissueItem: record %s event: %w", reason, err)
384		}
385	
386		// Issue the replacement copy.
387		newEvt := &models.DistributionEvent{
388			OrderID:     orderIDPtr,
388			MaterialID:  materialID,
389			Qty:         1,
390			EventType:   "issued",
391			ScanID:      &newScanID,
392			ActorID:     actorIDPtr,
393			CustodyFrom: stringPtr("clerk"),
394			CustodyTo:   stringPtr("student"),
395		}
396		if _, err := s.distRepo.RecordEvent(newEvt); err != nil {
397			return fmt.Errorf("service: ReissueItem: record replacement issue event: %w", err)
398		}
399	
400		observability.Distribution.Info("item reissued", "old_scan_id", oldScanID, "new_scan_id", newScanID, "reason", reason)
401		return nil
402	}

683	// pending_payment → pending_shipment and inserts a financial_transaction row
684	// of type "receipt" in the same database transaction, so the audit trail is
685	// never broken. If the financial record cannot be written the status change is
686	// rolled back and the error returned.
687	func (r *OrderRepository) ConfirmPaymentWithReceipt(orderID, actorID int64) error {
687		tx, err := r.db.Begin()
688		if err != nil {
689			return fmt.Errorf("repository: ConfirmPaymentWithReceipt: begin: %w", err)
690		}
691		defer tx.Rollback() //nolint:errcheck
692	
693		// Read current status and total_amount in one query.
694		var fromStatus string
695		var totalAmount float64
696		if err := tx.QueryRow(
697			`SELECT status, total_amount FROM orders WHERE id = ?`, orderID,
697		).Scan(&fromStatus, &totalAmount); err != nil {
698			if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
699			return fmt.Errorf("repository: ConfirmPaymentWithReceipt: order %d not found", orderID)
700			}
700		return fmt.Errorf("repository: ConfirmPaymentWithReceipt: load order: %w", err)
701		}
702		if fromStatus != "pending_payment" {
702		return fmt.Errorf("repository: ConfirmPaymentWithReceipt: order is %q, expected pending_payment", fromStatus)
703		}
703	
704		// Advance status to pending_shipment (auto_close_at extended by 72 h).
704		if _, err := tx.Exec(`
705			UPDATE orders
706			SET    status        = 'pending_shipment',
707			       auto_close_at = datetime('now', '+72 hours'),
708			       updated_at    = datetime('now')
709		WHERE  id = ?`, orderID); err != nil {
710			return fmt.Errorf("repository: ConfirmPaymentWithReceipt: update order: %w", err)
710		}
711	
712		// Insert order_event.
712		note := "payment confirmed"
713		if _, err := tx.Exec(`
713			INSERT INTO order_events (order_id, from_status, to_status, actor_id, note)
714			VALUES (?, 'pending_payment', 'pending_shipment', ?, ?)`,
714			orderID, actorID, note); err != nil {
715		return fmt.Errorf("repository: ConfirmPaymentWithReceipt: insert order_event: %w", err)
715		}
716	
717		// Insert the financial receipt — links the payment to the order for audit.
717		ref := fmt.Sprintf("ORDER-%d", orderID)
718		if _, err := tx.Exec(`
718			INSERT INTO financial_transactions
719			            (order_id, type, amount, status, reference, note, actor_id)
720			VALUES      (?, 'receipt', ?, 'completed', ?, 'payment received', ?)`,
720			orderID, totalAmount, ref, actorID); err != nil {
721		return fmt.Errorf("repository: ConfirmPaymentWithReceipt: insert financial_transaction: %w", err)
721		}
722	
723		return tx.Commit()
724	}
725	
726	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
727	// Auto-close (scheduler entry point)
728	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
729	
730	// CloseOverdueOrders cancels all orders whose auto_close_at has passed and
731	// whose status is pending_payment or pending_shipment. Returns the count of
732	// orders closed.
733	func (r *OrderRepository) CloseOverdueOrders(materialRepo *MaterialRepository) (int, error) {
733		const selectQ = `
733		SELECT id, status
734			FROM   orders
735			WHERE  status IN ('pending_payment', 'pending_shipment')
736			  AND  auto_close_at IS NOT NULL
737			  AND  auto_close_at < datetime('now')`
737	
738		rows, err := r.db.Query(selectQ)
738		if err != nil {
739			return 0, fmt.Errorf("repository: CloseOverdueOrders: query: %w", err)
740		}
741		type row struct {
741		id     int64
742			status string
743		}
744		var overdue []row
745		for rows.Next() {
745		var rw row
746			if err := rows.Scan(&rw.id, &rw.status); err != nil {
747				rows.Close()
748				return 0, err
749			}
750			overdue = append(overdue, rw)
751		}
752		rows.Close()
752		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
753			return 0, err
754		}
754	
755		closed := 0
755		for _, rw := range overdue {
756		note := "auto-closed: payment timeout"
757			if rw.status == "pending_shipment" {
758				note = "auto-closed: shipment timeout"
759			}
760		if err := r.Transition(rw.id, 0, "canceled", note, materialRepo); err != nil {
760			// Log but continue — don't abort the whole batch on a single failure.
761			continue
762		}
763			closed++
764		}
765	
766		return closed, nil
767	}
768	
769	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
769	// helpers
770	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
771	
772	type orderScanner interface {
772		Scan(dest ...interface{}) error
773	}

226			VALUES (?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`

228		if err != nil {

233		const countQ = `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT reported_by) FROM comment_reports WHERE comment_id = ?`
234		err = tx.QueryRow(countQ, commentID).Scan(&uniqueReporters)
235		if err != nil {

241			if _, err = tx.Exec(collapseQ, commentID); err != nil {

35	// GetPendingReview returns paginated comments whose status is 'collapsed',
36	// enriched with report counts, reporter usernames, material title, and author name.
37	func (r *ModerationRepository) GetPendingReview(limit, offset int) ([]ModerationItem, error) {
38		// Fetch collapsed comments with their material title and author name.
39		const q = `
40			SELECT c.id, c.material_id, c.user_id, c.body, c.link_count,
41			       c.status, c.report_count, c.created_at, c.updated_at,
42			       m.title      AS material_title,
43			       u.username   AS author_name
44			FROM   comments c
45			JOIN   materials m ON m.id = c.material_id
46			JOIN   users     u ON u.id = c.user_id
47			WHERE  c.status = 'collapsed'
48			ORDER  BY c.report_count DESC, c.created_at ASC
49			LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
50	
51		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, limit, offset)
52		if err != nil {
53			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: %w", err)
54		}
55		defer rows.Close()
56	
57		type rawRow struct {
58			comment       models.Comment
59			materialTitle string
60			authorName    string
61		}
62		var raws []rawRow
63	
64		for rows.Next() {
65			var rw rawRow
66			c := &rw.comment
67			if err := rows.Scan(
68				&c.ID, &c.MaterialID, &c.UserID, &c.Body, &c.LinkCount,
69				&c.Status, &c.ReportCount, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt,
70				&rw.materialTitle, &rw.authorName,
71			); err != nil {
72				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: scan: %w", err)
73			}
74			raws = append(raws, rw)
75		}
76		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
77			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: rows: %w", err)
78		}
79	
80		// For each comment, fetch the reporter usernames.
81		const reportersQ = `
82			SELECT u.username
83			FROM   comment_reports cr
84			JOIN   users u ON u.id = cr.reported_by
85			WHERE  cr.comment_id = ?
86			ORDER  BY cr.created_at ASC`
87	
88		items := make([]ModerationItem, 0, len(raws))
89		for _, rw := range raws {
90			rptRows, err := r.db.Query(reportersQ, rw.comment.ID)
91			if err != nil {
92				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: reporters query: %w", err)
93			}
94			var reporters []string
95			for rptRows.Next() {
96				var uname string
97				if err := rptRows.Scan(&uname); err != nil {
98					rptRows.Close()
99					return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: reporters scan: %w", err)
100				}
101				reporters = append(reporters, uname)
102			}
103			rptRows.Close()
104			if err := rptRows.Err(); err != nil {
105				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.GetPendingReview: reporters rows: %w", err)
106			}
107	
108			items = append(items, ModerationItem{
109				Comment:       rw.comment,
110				ReportCount:   rw.comment.ReportCount,
111				Reporters:     reporters,
112				MaterialTitle: rw.materialTitle,
113				AuthorName:    rw.authorName,
114			})
115		}
116		return items, nil
117	}
118	
119	// ApproveComment sets a comment's status to 'active'. Reports are kept for audit.
120	func (r *ModerationRepository) ApproveComment(commentID, moderatorID int64) error {
121		const q = `
122			UPDATE comments
123			SET    status     = 'active',
124			       updated_at = datetime('now')
125			WHERE  id = ? AND status = 'collapsed'`
126	
127		res, err := r.db.Exec(q, commentID)
128		if err != nil {
129			return fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.ApproveComment: %w", err)
130		}
131		n, err := res.RowsAffected()
132		if err != nil {
133			return fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.ApproveComment: rows affected: %w", err)
134		}
135		if n == 0 {
136			return fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.ApproveComment: comment %d: %w", commentID, ErrCommentNotReviewable)
137		}
138		return nil
139	}
140	
141	// RemoveComment sets a comment's status to 'removed'.
142	func (r *ModerationRepository) RemoveComment(commentID, moderatorID int64) error {
143		const q = `
144			UPDATE comments
145			SET    status     = 'removed',
146			       updated_at = datetime('now')
147			WHERE  id = ? AND status = 'collapsed'`
148	
149		res, err := r.db.Exec(q, commentID)
150		if err != nil {
151			return fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.RemoveComment: %w", err)
152		}
153		n, err := res.RowsAffected()
154		if err != nil {
155			return fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.RemoveComment: rows affected: %w", err)
156		}
157		if n == 0 {
158			return fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.RemoveComment: comment %d: %w", commentID, ErrCommentNotReviewable)
159		}
160		return nil
161	}
162	
163	// CountPending returns the number of comments awaiting moderation review.
164	func (r *ModerationRepository) CountPending() (int, error) {
165		const q = `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM comments WHERE status = 'collapsed'`
166	
167		var n int
168		if err := r.db.QueryRow(q).Scan(&n); err != nil {
169			return 0, fmt.Errorf("repository: ModerationRepository.CountPending: %w", err)
170		}
171		return n, nil
172	}

39	// TestApproveComment verifies POST /moderation/:id/approve transitions a
40	// collapsed comment to active status in the DB.
41	func TestApproveComment(t *testing.T) {
42		app, db, cleanup := newTestApp(t)
43		defer cleanup()
44	
45		// Create a comment author.
46		author := createTestUser(t, db, "student")
47		mat := createTestMaterial(t, db)
48	
49		// Create comment and collapse it (set status directly).
50		engRepo := repository.NewEngagementRepository(db)
51		comment, err := engRepo.CreateComment(mat.ID, author.ID, "This needs review", 0)
52		if err != nil {
53			t.Fatalf("create comment: %v", err)
54		}
55	
56		// Set it to collapsed directly in DB.
57		if _, err := db.Exec(`UPDATE comments SET status = 'collapsed' WHERE id = ?`, comment.ID); err != nil {
58			t.Fatalf("set collapsed: %v", err)
59		}
60	
61		modCookie := loginAs(t, app, db, "moderator")
62	
63		resp := makeRequest(app, http.MethodPost,
64			fmt.Sprintf("/moderation/%d/approve", comment.ID),
65			"", modCookie, "application/x-www-form-urlencoded", htmxHeaders())
66	
67		// On HTMX request, the handler returns 200 with empty body.
68		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound {
69			t.Fatalf("expected 200 or 302 on approve, got %d; body: %s",
70				resp.StatusCode, readBody(resp))
71		}
72	
73		// Verify DB.
74		var status string
75		if err := db.QueryRow(`SELECT status FROM comments WHERE id = ?`, comment.ID).Scan(&status); err != nil {
76			t.Fatalf("query comment: %v", err)
77		}
78		if status != "active" {
79			t.Errorf("expected comment status 'active' after approve, got %q", status)
80		}
81	}

345	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
346	// Reissue
347	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
348	
349	// ReissueItem handles lost or damaged copy replacement:
350	//   - Records a "lost" or "damaged" event for oldScanID (marking it out of
351	//     circulation).
352	//   - Records a new "issued" event for newScanID.
353	//
354	// reason must be "lost" or "damaged".
355	func (s *DistributionService) ReissueItem(orderID, materialID, actorID int64, oldScanID, newScanID, reason string) error {
356		if reason != "lost" && reason != "damaged" {
357			return fmt.Errorf("service: ReissueItem: invalid reason %q (must be 'lost' or 'damaged')", reason)
358		}
359		if oldScanID == "" || newScanID == "" {
360			return errors.New("service: ReissueItem: both oldScanID and newScanID are required")
361		}
362	
363		// Verify order exists.
364		if _, err := s.orderRepo.GetByID(orderID); err != nil {
365			if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
366				return fmt.Errorf("service: ReissueItem: order %d not found", orderID)
367			}
368			return fmt.Errorf("service: ReissueItem: load order: %w", err)
369		}
370	
371		orderIDPtr := &orderID
372		actorIDPtr := &actorID
373	
374		// Record the loss/damage event for the old scan ID.
375		oldEvt := &models.DistributionEvent{
376			OrderID:    orderIDPtr,
377			MaterialID: materialID,
378			Qty:        1,
379			EventType:  reason, // "lost" or "damaged"
380			ScanID:     &oldScanID,
381			ActorID:    actorIDPtr,
382		}
383		if _, err := s.distRepo.RecordEvent(oldEvt); err != nil {
384			return fmt.Errorf("service: ReissueItem: record %s event: %w", reason, err)
385		}
386	
387		// Issue the replacement copy.
388		newEvt := &models.DistributionEvent{
389			OrderID:     orderIDPtr,
390			MaterialID:  materialID,
391			Qty:         1,
392			EventType:   "issued",
393			ScanID:      &newScanID,
394			ActorID:     actorIDPtr,
395			CustodyFrom: stringPtr("clerk"),
396			CustodyTo:   stringPtr("student"),
397		}
398		if _, err := s.distRepo.RecordEvent(newEvt); err != nil {
399			return fmt.Errorf("service: ReissueItem: record replacement issue event: %w", err)
400		}
401	
402		observability.Distribution.Info("item reissued", "old_scan_id", oldScanID, "new_scan_id", newScanID, "reason", reason)
403		return nil
404	}

33			m.TotalQty, m.AvailableQty, m.ReservedQty, m.Price, m.Status,

content.text:
# HTMX Library Content

The provided content is the **complete minified htmx JavaScript library** (version 2.0.4). Here's what it contains:

## Overview
This is the production-ready, minified version of htmx—a lightweight JavaScript library enabling declarative AJAX interactions directly through HTML attributes.

## Key Features Included
- **AJAX Operations**: Core methods for GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH requests
- **DOM Manipulation**: Element selection, class toggling, content swapping
- **Event Handling**: Custom event triggering and listener management
- **Form Processing**: Input validation, form data collection and encoding
- **History Management**: Browser history state tracking and navigation
- **Swap Strategies**: Multiple content replacement approaches (innerHTML, outerHTML, beforeend, etc.)
- **Trigger Specifications**: Complex event parsing and polling mechanisms
- **Extension System**: Plugin architecture for custom functionality

## Technical Details
The library is wrapped in a self-executing function returning a configuration object (`Q`) with approximately 40+ public methods and extensive internal utilities. Configuration includes history caching, swap delays, CSS class management, and security settings.

All functionality operates through HTML5 attributes like `hx-get`, `hx-target`, `hx-swap`, enabling progressive enhancement without explicit JavaScript.

# Alpine.js Library Analysis

The content you've provided is the **minified Alpine.js v3.14.3**, a lightweight JavaScript framework for building reactive user interfaces.

## Key Components

**Core Reactivity System:**
The library implements a reactive proxy system using JavaScript Proxies and WeakMaps to track object mutations and trigger effects when data changes.

**Directive System:**
Alpine provides directives like `x-data`, `x-bind`, `x-on`, `x-for`, `x-if`, `x-model`, `x-show`, and `x-transition` that bind DOM elements to reactive state and handle user interactions.

**Magic Properties:**
Special properties prefixed with `$` provide utilities like `$nextTick`, `$dispatch`, `$watch`, and `$el` for DOM manipulation and event handling.

**Transition System:**
Built-in support for CSS transitions with customizable timing, easing, and animation properties.

**Key Features:**
- DOM mutation observation for automatic reinitialization
- Effect scheduling and cleanup
- Template cloning for `x-for` loops
- Teleportation of DOM elements
- Model binding with modifiers (debounce, throttle, lazy, etc.)
- Two-way data synchronization

The library is self-initializing and automatically processes Alpine directives when `document.body` is available, making it suitable for progressive enhancement of HTML documents.

# HTMX SSE Extension Overview

This JavaScript extension integrates Server Sent Events (SSE) functionality into HTMX. The code defines an extension that manages bidirectional communication between SSE connections and DOM elements.

## Key Components

**Core Functionality:**
The extension registers itself with HTMX and provides handlers for two main attributes: `sse-connect` (establishes SSE connections) and `sse-swap` (listens for specific server events).

**Event Management:**
The implementation creates EventSource objects that remain open and listen for named events. When events arrive, the extension automatically swaps content into targeted DOM elements using HTMX's standard swap mechanisms.

**Connection Lifecycle:**
The code includes automatic reconnection logic with exponential backoff (capped at 128 seconds). When elements are removed from the DOM, associated event sources are properly cleaned up.

**Trigger Integration:**
Beyond `sse-swap`, the extension also supports `hx-trigger` attributes using the `sse:` prefix, allowing SSE events to trigger HTMX requests or custom behaviors.

**Error Handling:**
The extension emits custom events (`htmx:sseError`, `htmx:sseOpen`, `htmx:sseClose`) that applications can monitor for connection issues and state changes.

The implementation demonstrates defensive programming through repeated checks ensuring elements remain in the document before operating on them.

Good — `.env` was never tracked. Now update the README to clarify `.env.example` workflow and document the vendored assets:

1	# District Materials Commerce & Logistics Portal
2	
3	A web-based portal for managing district-wide distribution of educational materials. It provides role-aware workflows for students (browsing, ordering, favorites), instructors (course plans, approvals), clerks (distribution, ledger), moderators (comment queue), and administrators (users, analytics, settings). Built with Go, Fiber, SQLite, HTMX, and Alpine.js.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Go 1.22 or later
8	- GCC (required to compile the `mattn/go-sqlite3` CGo driver)
9	  - macOS: `xcode-select --install`
10	  - Ubuntu/Debian: `sudo apt install gcc`
11	  - Windows: install [TDM-GCC](https://jmeubank.github.io/tdm-gcc/) or use WSL
12	
13	## Setup
14	
15	1. **Clone the repository**
16	
17	   ```bash
18	   git clone <repo-url>
19	   cd w2t86
20	   ```
21	
22	2. **Install Go dependencies**
23	
24	   ```bash
25	   go mod tidy
26	   ```
27	
28	3. **Download HTMX and Alpine.js**
29	
30	   Place the minified files in `web/static/js/`:
31	
32	   ```bash
33	   # HTMX 2.x
34	   curl -Lo web/static/js/htmx.min.js \
35	     https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2/dist/htmx.min.js
36	
37	   # Alpine.js 3.x
38	   curl -Lo web/static/js/alpine.min.js \
39	     https://unpkg.com/alpinejs@3/dist/cdn.min.js
40	   ```
41	
42	4. **Configure environment**
43	
44	   Create a `.env` file in the project root (it is gitignored — never commit it):
45	
46	   ```bash
47	   # Generate the two required secrets
48	   echo "ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
49	   echo "SESSION_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
50	
51	   # Add the remaining variables with your preferred values
52	   echo "PORT=3000"            >> .env
53	   echo "DB_PATH=data/portal.db" >> .env
54	   echo "APP_ENV=development"  >> .env
55	   echo "BANNED_WORDS="        >> .env
56	   ```
57	
58	   See the [Environment Variables](#environment-variables) section below for a
59	   description of every variable.
60	
61	5. **Run the server**
62	
63	   ```bash
64	   go run -tags sqlite_fts5 ./cmd/server
65	   ```
66	
67	   The server listens on `http://localhost:3000` by default.
68	
69	## Docker
70	
71	### Quick start
72	```bash
73	# Create .env with generated secrets (gitignored — never commit this file)
74	echo "ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >  .env
75	echo "SESSION_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
76	echo "PORT=3000"             >> .env
77	echo "DB_PATH=/app/data/portal.db" >> .env
78	echo "APP_ENV=production"    >> .env
79	
80	docker compose up -d
81	```
82	
83	### Development (with live template reload)
84	```bash
85	docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml up
86	```
87	
88	### Useful commands
89	```bash
90	make docker-logs     # tail logs
91	make docker-down     # stop
92	make test            # run tests locally
93	```
94	
95	## Environment Variables
96	
97	The application reads all configuration from environment variables.
98	A **template `.env` file** is committed to the repository with placeholder
99	values only — no real secrets are stored in it.  Copy it to a local `.env`,
100	replace the placeholder values, and the file will be ignored by git.
101	
102	### Minimal `.env` for local development
103	
104	```dotenv
105	# --- Required secrets (generate with: openssl rand -hex 32) ---
106	ENCRYPTION_KEY=<64-hex-char string, 32 bytes>
107	SESSION_SECRET=<long random string>
108	
109	# --- Optional: shown here with their default values ---
110	PORT=3000

110	PORT=3000
111	DB_PATH=data/portal.db
112	APP_ENV=development
113	BANNED_WORDS=
114	```
115	
116	### Variable reference
117	
118	| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
119	|---|---|---|---|
120	| `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | **yes** | — | 64-character hex string (32 bytes) used as the AES-256-GCM key for encrypting sensitive user custom fields. Generate with `openssl rand -hex 32`. |
121	| `SESSION_SECRET` | **yes** | — | Arbitrary secret string used to sign and verify session tokens. Use a long random value; `openssl rand -hex 32` is sufficient. |
122	| `PORT` | no | `3000` | TCP port the HTTP server binds to. |
123	| `DB_PATH` | no | `data/portal.db` | Filesystem path to the SQLite database file. The parent directory is created automatically on first run. In Docker the volume is mounted at `/app/data`, so use `/app/data/portal.db`. |
124	| `APP_ENV` | no | `development` | Runtime environment. Set to `production` to disable template hot-reload and enable stricter security defaults. Any other value is treated as development. |
125	| `BANNED_WORDS` | no | *(empty)* | Comma-separated list of words blocked in material comments (e.g. `spam,abuse`). Leave empty to disable the filter entirely. |
126	
127	## Default Credentials
128	
129	| Username | Password       | Role  |
130	|----------|----------------|-------|
131	| `admin`  | `ChangeMe123!` | admin |
132	
133	**Change the admin password immediately after first login.**
134	
135	The default admin account is inserted by the initial migration. Update it via the Admin Settings page or directly in the database.
136	
137	## Available Roles
138	
139	| Role         | Capabilities                                                        |
140	|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
141	| `student`    | Browse materials, place orders, manage favorites, inbox             |
142	| `instructor` | Course plans, approve orders, inbox                                 |
143	| `clerk`      | Distribution events, ledger, backorder management, inbox            |
144	| `moderator`  | Review and act on reported comments, inbox                          |
145	| `admin`      | Full access: user management, analytics, all settings, all of above |
146	
147	## Project Structure
148	
149	```
150	w2t86/
151	├── cmd/
152	│   └── server/
153	│       └── main.go              # Entry point: wires and starts the server
154	├── internal/
155	│   ├── config/
156	│   │   └── config.go            # Environment-based configuration
157	│   ├── crypto/
158	│   │   └── crypto.go            # Password hashing + AES-256-GCM helpers
159	│   ├── db/
160	│   │   └── db.go                # SQLite open + migration runner
161	│   ├── handlers/
162	│   │   └── auth.go              # Login / logout HTTP handlers
163	│   ├── middleware/
164	│   │   ├── auth.go              # Session validation, GetUser helper
165	│   │   ├── ratelimit.go         # Sliding-window rate limiter
166	│   │   └── rbac.go              # Role-based access control
167	│   ├── models/
168	│   │   └── models.go            # Go structs for every DB table
169	│   ├── repository/
170	│   │   ├── sessions.go          # Session CRUD
171	│   │   └── users.go             # User CRUD
172	│   ├── scheduler/
173	│   │   └── scheduler.go         # Cron jobs: auto-close stale orders
174	│   └── services/
175	│       └── auth.go              # Login, logout, register business logic
176	├── migrations/
177	│   └── 001_schema.sql           # Full database schema
178	├── web/
179	│   ├── static/
180	│   │   ├── css/
181	│   │   │   └── app.css          # Hand-crafted application styles
182	│   │   └── js/
183	│   │       ├── app.js           # Vanilla JS utilities (toast, confirm, etc.)
184	│   │       ├── alpine.min.js    # Alpine.js 3.x (download separately)
185	│   │       └── htmx.min.js      # HTMX 2.x (download separately)
186	│   └── templates/
187	│       ├── layouts/
188	│       │   ├── base.html        # Full layout: sidebar + topbar (authenticated)
189	│       │   └── main.html        # Minimal layout: HTML shell (login page)
190	│       ├── pages/               # (reserved for future page templates)
191	│       ├── partials/
192	│       │   ├── login_form.html  # Login form partial (HTMX swap target)
193	│       │   └── toast.html       # Toast notification partial (hx-swap-oob)
194	│       ├── login.html           # Login page content
195	│       └── dashboard.html       # Dashboard page content
196	├── .env                         # Local secrets — gitignored, never committed
197	├── go.mod
198	├── go.sum
199	└── README.md
200	```
201

All 17 test packages pass. Here's what was done for each audit finding:

---

### Blocker — Frontend dependencies were placeholders
All 8 libraries are now **fully vendored** into `web/static/`:

| Asset | Version | Size |
|---|---|---|
| `htmx.min.js` | 2.0.4 | 51 KB |
| `alpine.min.js` | 3.14.3 | 45 KB |
| `bootstrap.bundle.min.js` | 5.3.3 | 81 KB |
| `leaflet.js` | 1.9.4 | 148 KB |
| `htmx-ext-sse.js` | 2.2.2 | 9 KB |
| `bootstrap.min.css` | 5.3.3 | 233 KB |
| `bootstrap-icons.min.css` + fonts | 1.11.3 | 86 KB + 306 KB |
| `leaflet.css` + images | 1.9.4 | 15 KB + 5 images |

`make vendor-assets` target added for future updates.

### High — `/inbox/sse` missing from production route table (`main.go:366`)
Added `app.Get("/inbox/sse", requireAuth, messagingHandler.InboxSSE)` without `csrfMiddleware` (GET is safe; EventSource cannot set custom headers). New test `TestInboxSSE_RouteRegistered` confirms 404 is impossible.

### High — Collapsed comments exposed to non-moderators (`engagement.go:184-189`)
Changed `WHERE material_id = ? AND status != 'removed'` → `WHERE material_id = ? AND status = 'visible'`. Explicit allowlist instead of denylist — future status values can't accidentally leak. New test `TestCollapsedComment_HiddenFromNonModerator` asserts student sees no collapsed content, moderator sees all.

### High — Moderation approve breaks re-collapse cycle (`moderation.go:119-125`)
Changed `status = 'active'` → `status = 'visible'` in `ApproveComment`. The `ReportComment` collapse predicate (`WHERE status = 'visible'`) now correctly matches approved comments. New test `TestModeration_ReCollapseAfterApproval` exercises the full cycle: collapse → approve → re-report → auto-collapse.

### High — Reissue records events without checking/decrementing inventory (`distribution.go:355-404`)
`ReissueItem` now:
1. Loads the material and rejects if `available_qty < 1` (mirrors `RecordExchange`)
2. Calls `DirectIssue(materialID, 1)` after recording the replacement event
Two new tests: `TestReissue_ZeroStock_Fails` and `TestReissue_DecrementsInventory`.

### Medium — `.env` confusion
Created `.env.example` (tracked, placeholder values), confirmed `.env` was never tracked by git, updated README setup steps to `cp .env.example .env`, and updated the Docker quick-start section.

## 1. Verdict
- Overall conclusion: **Fail**

## 2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
- What was reviewed:
  - Docs/config/scripts: `repo/README.md:1`, `repo/Makefile:1`, `repo/run_tests.sh:1`, `repo/.env.example:1`
  - Entry points/routes/middleware: `repo/cmd/server/main.go:1`, `repo/internal/middleware/auth.go:1`, `repo/internal/middleware/rbac.go:1`
  - Core services/repositories/models/migrations: `repo/internal/services/*.go`, `repo/internal/repository/*.go`, `repo/internal/models/models.go:1`, `repo/migrations/*.sql`
  - Tests/logging: `repo/unit_tests/*`, `repo/API_tests/*`, `repo/internal/integration/*`, `repo/internal/observability/*`
- What was not reviewed:
  - Runtime behavior, browser behavior, performance under load, deployment environment correctness.
- What was intentionally not executed:
  - Project start/run, tests, Docker, DB migrations at runtime.
- Claims requiring manual verification:
  - HTMX/SSE “live push” UX continuity in real browser sessions.
  - Geospatial rendering efficiency at high data volume.
  - End-to-end offline tile/boundary operational behavior in target on-prem environment.

## 3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
- Prompt core goal mapped: role-based district materials planning/order/distribution/after-sales portal with moderation, messaging, analytics, geospatial, auditable state transitions.
- Main implementation areas mapped:
  - Auth/RBAC/session/lockout/password policy (`repo/internal/services/auth.go:20`, `repo/cmd/server/main.go:303`)
  - Materials/engagement (rating/comments/favorites/share/history) (`repo/internal/services/materials.go:16`)
  - Orders/state machine/returns/financial linkage/scheduler (`repo/internal/repository/orders.go:281`, `repo/internal/services/orders.go:171`, `repo/internal/scheduler/scheduler.go:11`)
  - Distribution ledger/custody/reissue (`repo/internal/services/distribution.go:46`)
  - Messaging + DND + subscriptions (`repo/internal/services/messaging.go:19`)
  - Analytics/export/geospatial (`repo/internal/services/analytics.go:15`)

## 4. Section-by-section Review

### 1. Hard Gates
- 1.1 Documentation and static verifiability
  - Conclusion: **Pass**
  - Rationale: Startup/config/test/build instructions exist and align with actual entry points and scripts.
  - Evidence: `repo/README.md:13`, `repo/README.md:67`, `repo/Makefile:3`, `repo/cmd/server/main.go:1`, `repo/run_tests.sh:4`
- 1.2 Material deviation from Prompt
  - Conclusion: **Fail**
  - Rationale: Two prompt-critical behaviors are materially broken/under-enforced:
    - Distribution return/exchange does not validate returned material against ordered/issued lines.
    - KPI monetary metrics are undermined because price is authoritative in order math but missing from admin material create/edit flow.
  - Evidence: `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:202`, `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:272`, `repo/internal/repository/orders.go:79`, `repo/internal/handlers/materials.go:611`, `repo/web/templates/admin/materials/new.html:92`, `repo/web/templates/admin/materials/edit.html:101`, `repo/migrations/016_material_price.sql:6`

### 2. Delivery Completeness
- 2.1 Core requirement coverage
  - Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
  - Rationale: Most major flows exist (roles, comments/rating, favorites sharing, order lifecycle, returns/exchanges, messaging, dashboards, geospatial), but critical gaps above block full acceptance.
  - Evidence: `repo/cmd/server/main.go:325`, `repo/internal/services/materials.go:159`, `repo/internal/repository/orders.go:281`, `repo/internal/services/messaging.go:31`, `repo/internal/services/analytics.go:304`
- 2.2 End-to-end 0→1 deliverable shape
  - Conclusion: **Pass**
  - Rationale: Coherent multi-module app with migrations, templates, handlers, services, repos, docs, and tests.
  - Evidence: `repo/README.md:152`, `repo/migrations/001_schema.sql:1`, `repo/internal/handlers/orders.go:1`, `repo/API_tests/helpers_test.go:1`

### 3. Engineering and Architecture Quality
- 3.1 Structure and modularity
  - Conclusion: **Pass**
  - Rationale: Layered decomposition is clear and responsibilities are mostly separated.
  - Evidence: `repo/README.md:152`, `repo/internal/handlers/errors.go:9`, `repo/internal/services/orders.go:13`, `repo/internal/repository/orders.go:19`
- 3.2 Maintainability/extensibility
  - Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
  - Rationale: Generally maintainable, but key business invariants are not centralized strongly enough (distribution return/exchange material-line integrity), increasing extension risk.
  - Evidence: `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:202`, `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:272`

### 4. Engineering Details and Professionalism
- 4.1 Error handling/logging/validation/API quality
  - Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
  - Rationale: Strong generic error hygiene and logging categories exist, but there are security/data-exposure and authorization-policy leaks in specific endpoints.
  - Evidence: `repo/internal/handlers/errors.go:31`, `repo/internal/observability/request_logger.go:33`, `repo/cmd/server/main.go:231`, `repo/internal/handlers/analytics.go:322`
- 4.2 Product-level credibility
  - Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
  - Rationale: Looks like a real service, but high-severity defects in inventory integrity and KPI pricing reduce production credibility.
  - Evidence: `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:202`, `repo/internal/handlers/materials.go:611`, `repo/internal/repository/orders.go:79`

### 5. Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit
- 5.1 Business understanding and fit
  - Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
  - Rationale: Broad understanding is strong (role workflows, moderation, state machine, returns, DND, geospatial, exports), but crucial semantics around controlled inventory/funds integrity and analytics monetization are incompletely enforced.
  - Evidence: `repo/internal/services/orders.go:229`, `repo/internal/repository/orders.go:638`, `repo/internal/services/analytics.go:304`, `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:202`

### 6. Aesthetics (frontend/full-stack)
- 6.1 Visual/interaction quality
  - Conclusion: **Cannot Confirm Statistically**
  - Rationale: Static templates/styles indicate structured layouts and interaction hooks, but final visual quality and interaction fidelity require runtime/browser verification.
  - Evidence: `repo/web/templates/layouts/base.html:1`, `repo/web/templates/materials/detail.html:1`, `repo/web/static/css/app.css:1`
  - Manual verification note: Verify responsive behavior, HTMX swaps, and SSE refresh usability in a browser.

## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)

### Blocker / High
1. **Severity: High**
- Title: Distribution return/exchange flow can mutate inventory for materials not tied to the order line
- Conclusion: **Fail**
- Evidence: `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:202`, `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:222`, `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:236`, `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:272`, `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:296`
- Impact: Approved return/exchange requests can be used to inflate/alter stock for arbitrary materials, breaking ledger traceability and audit integrity.
- Minimum actionable fix: Validate `materialID`/`oldMaterialID` against `order_items` (and issued quantities) before restocking or exchanging; reject mismatches and over-returns.

2. **Severity: High**
- Title: `/api/stats/:stat` leaks sensitive operational metrics to non-admin users
- Conclusion: **Fail**
- Evidence: `repo/cmd/server/main.go:370`, `repo/internal/handlers/analytics.go:322`, `repo/internal/services/analytics.go:411`, `repo/internal/services/analytics.go:417`, `repo/internal/services/analytics.go:426`
- Impact: Authenticated non-admin roles can query admin-sensitive counts (`pending-issues`, `moderation-queue`, `backorders`, etc.), violating least-privilege data boundaries.
- Minimum actionable fix: Enforce an explicit per-stat role allowlist (or split admin-only stats into admin-only route group).

3. **Severity: High**
- Title: Monetary KPI pipeline is likely invalid because material `price` is authoritative but not settable in admin UI/form parsing
- Conclusion: **Fail**
- Evidence: `repo/internal/repository/orders.go:79`, `repo/migrations/016_material_price.sql:6`, `repo/internal/handlers/materials.go:611`, `repo/internal/handlers/materials.go:643`, `repo/web/templates/admin/materials/new.html:92`, `repo/web/templates/admin/materials/edit.html:101`
- Impact: Orders can compute with default `price=0`, making GMV/AOV/conversion economics unreliable.
- Minimum actionable fix: Add `price` input and validation in create/edit templates and handler parsing; require non-negative (or positive) price policy.

### Medium
4. **Severity: Medium**
- Title: Public health endpoint returns raw DB error text
- Conclusion: **Partial Fail**
- Evidence: `repo/cmd/server/main.go:231`
- Impact: Operational internals may leak in error payloads.
- Minimum actionable fix: Return generic unhealthy response body; keep detailed DB error in logs only.

5. **Severity: Medium**
- Title: Test coverage misses newly identified high-risk authorization/integrity paths
- Conclusion: **Partial Fail**
- Evidence: `repo/internal/services/distribution_test.go:59`, `repo/API_tests/permissions_test.go:18`, `repo/API_tests/permissions_test.go:121`, `repo/API_tests/permissions_test.go:219`
- Impact: Severe defects can remain undetected while suites still pass.
- Minimum actionable fix: Add negative tests for cross-material return/exchange validation and non-admin access to sensitive `/api/stats/:stat` values.

## 6. Security Review Summary
- Authentication entry points: **Pass**
  - Evidence: login + lockout + password constraints + session hashing present (`repo/internal/services/auth.go:20`, `repo/internal/middleware/auth.go:36`, `repo/internal/repository/sessions.go:21`).
- Route-level authorization: **Partial Pass**
  - Evidence: broad RBAC in route wiring (`repo/cmd/server/main.go:375` onward), but `/api/stats/:stat` route is only auth-gated with partial in-handler restriction (`repo/cmd/server/main.go:370`, `repo/internal/handlers/analytics.go:322`).
- Object-level authorization: **Fail**
  - Evidence: strong examples exist (`repo/internal/handlers/orders.go:65`, `repo/internal/handlers/materials.go:470`), but distribution returns/exchanges do not validate material-order binding (`repo/internal/services/distribution.go:202`, `repo/internal/services/distribution.go:272`).
- Function-level authorization: **Partial Pass**
  - Evidence: many service checks exist (e.g., return approval manager role `repo/internal/services/orders.go:236`), but sensitive stat access not fully constrained by role.
- Tenant/user data isolation: **Partial Pass**
  - Evidence: user-scoped queries are common (`repo/internal/repository/messaging.go:49`, `repo/internal/repository/engagement.go:46`), but metrics endpoint issue above leaks cross-user operational data.
- Admin/internal/debug protection: **Pass**
  - Evidence: admin-only protections for `/metrics`, analytics map/export/admin routes (`repo/cmd/server/main.go:244`, `repo/cmd/server/main.go:446`). No debug route exposure found in runtime code.

## 7. Tests and Logging Review
- Unit tests: **Pass (with gaps)**
  - Exists and covers core units (auth, state machine, rate limit, inventory/comment rules).
  - Evidence: `repo/unit_tests/auth_test.go:45`, `repo/unit_tests/statemachine_test.go:89`, `repo/unit_tests/ratelimit_test.go:70`.
- API/integration tests: **Partial Pass**
  - Broad route/role coverage exists, but misses key high-risk paths identified in this audit.
  - Evidence: `repo/API_tests/permissions_test.go:18`, `repo/API_tests/orders_test.go:1`, `repo/internal/integration/security_test.go:1`.
- Logging categories/observability: **Pass**
  - Structured category loggers + request middleware + metrics exist.
  - Evidence: `repo/internal/observability/logger.go:12`, `repo/internal/observability/request_logger.go:33`, `repo/internal/observability/metrics.go:9`.
- Sensitive-data leakage risk in logs/responses: **Partial Pass**
  - Most handlers use sanitized error responses (`repo/internal/handlers/errors.go:31`), but `/health` exposes raw DB error text (`repo/cmd/server/main.go:233`).

## 8. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)

### 8.1 Test Overview
- Unit tests exist: yes (`repo/unit_tests/*`, `repo/internal/services/*_test.go`, `repo/internal/repository/*_test.go`).
- API/integration tests exist: yes (`repo/API_tests/*`, `repo/internal/integration/*`).
- Framework: Go standard `testing` with `go test` runner.
- Test entry points: `make test` and `run_tests.sh`.
- Docs/commands: present in scripts/make.
- Evidence: `repo/Makefile:24`, `repo/run_tests.sh:4`, `repo/run_tests.sh:50`.

### 8.2 Coverage Mapping Table
| Requirement / Risk Point | Mapped Test Case(s) | Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock | Coverage Assessment | Gap | Minimum Test Addition |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Auth min length + 5-fail lockout 15m | `repo/unit_tests/auth_test.go:45`, `repo/unit_tests/auth_test.go:129` | Explicit lockout count assertions | sufficient | None major | Add API-level lockout expiry boundary test |
| Order state machine + auto-close | `repo/unit_tests/statemachine_test.go:89`, `repo/unit_tests/statemachine_test.go:256` | Valid/invalid transitions and overdue closure | sufficient | None major | Add cancel-from-in_transit role matrix API test |
| Comments limits (500 chars, <=2 links, rate) | `repo/internal/services/materials_test.go:114`, `repo/internal/services/materials_test.go:127`, `repo/unit_tests/ratelimit_test.go:70` | Validation + limiter behavior | basically covered | Mixed unit/API path coverage | Add API test for 429 on comment burst |
| Report auto-collapse after 3 unique reports | `repo/internal/integration/materials_test.go:1` (report flow), repo logic in `repo/internal/repository/engagement.go:217` | Unique reporter counting | insufficient | No explicit 3-unique threshold assertion identified | Add deterministic repo/service test for 1/2/3 unique reports |
| Distribution return/exchange integrity | `repo/internal/services/distribution_test.go:78`, `repo/internal/services/distribution_test.go:110` | Happy-path only | missing | No negative test for material not in order or over-return | Add failing tests for invalid material/qty linkage |
| Role/route authorization | `repo/API_tests/permissions_test.go:18` onward | Many role allow/deny checks | basically covered | `/api/stats/:stat` sensitive stat matrix missing | Add student/instructor forbidden tests for all sensitive stats |
| Object-level auth (order ownership/favorites ownership) | `repo/internal/integration/orders_test.go:1`, `repo/internal/handlers/orders.go:65`, `repo/internal/handlers/materials.go:470` | Some owner checks in code; limited explicit tests | insufficient | Hard to prove broad object-level coverage statically | Add targeted API tests for cross-user order/favorites access |
| PII masking in exports | `repo/internal/services/analytics.go:304` | Masking functions applied in CSV generation | insufficient | No explicit masking assertion tests found | Add unit tests verifying masked output format |

### 8.3 Security Coverage Audit
- Authentication: **sufficiently covered** by unit/API tests for login/lockout/session.
- Route authorization: **basically covered**, but not complete for all sensitive routes (`/api/stats/:stat` gap).
- Object-level authorization: **insufficient**; high-risk distribution/material-line integrity lacks negative tests.
- Tenant/data isolation: **insufficient**; no targeted test proving non-admin cannot fetch sensitive stat classes.
- Admin/internal protection: **basically covered** for metrics/map/export/admin pages.

### 8.4 Final Coverage Judgment
- **Partial Pass**
- Major covered risks: auth lockout/session, state machine transitions, broad role RBAC checks.
- Uncovered risks that can let severe defects survive passing tests: distribution return/exchange material integrity and sensitive stats exposure to non-admin roles.

## 9. Final Notes
- Audit is static-only; no runtime claims were made.
- High-severity findings are root-cause issues with direct code evidence.
- Prioritize fixing issues 1–3 before acceptance, then backfill targeted tests for those exact paths.

21	func TestPermission_Moderation_StudentForbidden(t *testing.T) {
22		app, db, cleanup := newTestApp(t)

24

27		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusForbidden && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusUnauthorized {

29		}
30	}

http.StatusUnauthorized { 231 t.Fatalf("admin should be allowed to access /metrics, got %d", resp.StatusCode) 232 } 233 } 234 235 // TestPermission_Metrics_StudentForbidden verifies that a student This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: ## 1. Primary Request and Intent This is a multi-round audit remediation session. The current (third) audit round asks to fix all failing issues from a new static review report: 1. **High** — Distribution `RecordReturn`/`RecordExchange` allow inventory mutation for arbitrary materials not tied to the order line. Must validate `materialID`/`oldMaterialID` against `order_items` before restocking. 2. **High** — `/api/stats/:stat` leaks sensitive operational metrics (`pending-issues`, `backorders`, `moderation-queue`, `course-plans`, `pending-returns`) to non-admin authenticated users. Current `adminOnlyStats` map is incomplete. 3. **High** — Material `price` is authoritative in order math but not settable through the admin UI. `materialModelFromForm` and `materialFieldsFromForm` don't parse `price`; templates `new.html`/`edit.html` lack a price input. 4. **Medium** — `/health` endpoint returns raw `err.Error()` in the response body, leaking operational internals. 5. **Medium** — Test coverage gaps: no negative tests for cross-material return/exchange validation; no tests for non-admin access to sensitive stats. Previous rounds (now complete) fixed: frontend dependencies (vendored), `/inbox/sse` route registration, collapsed comment visibility, moderation approve lifecycle, reissue inventory check/decrement, `.env.example`, SSE handler, pricing authority, financial receipt persistence, CSRF tests. --- ## 2. Key Technical Concepts - Go/Fiber/SQLite layered architecture (Handlers → Services → Repositories) - `order_items` table as the authoritative record of what was ordered per-order - Distribution integrity: return/exchange must only touch materials that appear in `order_items` for that specific order - `GetItemsByOrderID(orderID)` already exists on `OrderRepository` — returns `[]models.OrderItem` which can be used to build a `materialID → qty` map for validation - Per-stat role allowlist pattern vs. blanket admin gate for `/api/stats/:stat` - `materialModelFromForm` (used by `CreateMaterial`) vs `materialFieldsFromForm` (used by `UpdateMaterial`) — both need `price` parsing - `floatFormValue` helper may need to be added (or `strconv.ParseFloat` inline) since current helpers only have `intFormValue` - Health endpoint leaking DB error via `err.Error()` in JSON — fix by logging error and returning generic body - `adminOnlyStats` map at `internal/handlers/analytics.go:322` currently only guards 4 stats; at least 5 more sensitive ones need guarding --- ## 3. Files and Code Sections ### `internal/services/distribution.go` (needs changes) - **Why important**: `RecordReturn` (line 202) and `RecordExchange` (line 272) both accept arbitrary `materialID`/`oldMaterialID` without checking they belong to the order's `order_items`. - **Required fix for RecordReturn**: After validating the return request, load order items and confirm `materialID` is present: ```go // Validate materialID belongs to this order's items. orderItems, err := s.orderRepo.GetItemsByOrderID(orderID) if err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("service: RecordReturn: load order items: %w", err) } found := false for _, oi := range orderItems { if oi.MaterialID == materialID { found = true break } } if !found { return fmt.Errorf("service: RecordReturn: material %d is not part of order %d", materialID, orderID) } ``` - **Required fix for RecordExchange**: Same validation for `oldMaterialID` against `order_items`. ### `internal/handlers/analytics.go` (needs changes) - **Why important**: `adminOnlyStats` map at line 322 is incomplete — many sensitive stats are not in it. - **Current state**: ```go var adminOnlyStats = map[string]bool{ "total-orders": true, "active-users": true, "conversion-rate": true, "repeat-purchase": true, } ``` - **Required fix**: Expand to a role-aware allowlist or add all sensitive stats: ```go var adminOnlyStats = map[string]bool{ "total-orders": true, "active-users": true, "conversion-rate": true, "repeat-purchase": true, "pending-issues": true, // clerk/admin operational data "backorders": true, // clerk/admin operational data "moderation-queue": true, // moderator/admin internal queue "course-plans": true, // instructor/admin data "pending-returns": true, // instructor/admin data } ``` ### `internal/handlers/materials.go` (needs changes) - **Why important**: Lines 611-651 — `materialModelFromForm` and `materialFieldsFromForm` don't parse `price`. - **Current `materialModelFromForm`**: ```go func materialModelFromForm(c *fiber.Ctx) *models.Material { m := &models.Material{ Title: c.FormValue("title"), TotalQty: intFormValue(c, "total_qty"), AvailableQty: intFormValue(c, "available_qty"), ReservedQty: 0, Status: c.FormValue("status"), // Price MISSING } ``` - **Required addition to `materialModelFromForm`**: ```go if v := c.FormValue("price"); v != "" { if p, err := strconv.ParseFloat(v, 64); err == nil && p >= 0 { m.Price = p } } ``` - **Required addition to `materialFieldsFromForm`**: Add `price` to float parsing: ```go if v := c.FormValue("price"); v != "" { if p, err := strconv.ParseFloat(v, 64); err == nil && p >= 0 { fields["price"] = p } } ``` ### `web/templates/admin/materials/new.html` (needs changes) - **Why important**: Missing price input — orders default to `price=0` making GMV/AOV unreliable. - **Required**: Add to the quantity row: ```html
$
``` ### `web/templates/admin/materials/edit.html` (needs changes) - **Why important**: Missing price input — edits cannot correct a zero price. - **Required**: Same price input added to the quantity row, pre-populated with `{{.Material.Price}}`. ### `cmd/server/main.go` (needs change) - **Why important**: Line 233 leaks raw DB error text in health response. - **Current**: ```go return c.Status(503).JSON(fiber.Map{"status": "unhealthy", "error": err.Error()}) ``` - **Required**: ```go observability.App.Error("health check DB ping failed", "error", err) return c.Status(503).JSON(fiber.Map{"status": "unhealthy"}) ``` ### `internal/services/distribution_test.go` (needs new tests) - **Required**: Negative tests for cross-material validation: ```go func TestDistributionService_RecordReturn_MaterialNotInOrder(t *testing.T) { ... } func TestDistributionService_RecordReturn_OverReturn(t *testing.T) { ... } ``` ### `API_tests/permissions_test.go` (needs new tests) - **Required**: Tests for sensitive stats access by non-admin: ```go func TestPermission_Stats_SensitiveStatsForbiddenForStudent(t *testing.T) { ... } func TestPermission_Stats_AdminCanAccessAllStats(t *testing.T) { ... } ``` --- ## 4. Errors and Fixes - **`markItemsFulfilled` wrong argument count** (LSP stale): After adding `orderedQtys` param, LSP showed stale diagnostics. Build confirmed no error. - **`TestIssueItems_ForgedQtyExceedsOrdered` failing**: After the qty forgery fix, `qty=9999` in form maps to `IssueItem.Qty` (now ignored by service). The service uses `IssuedQty=0` → defaults to `oqty=1`. Test was asserting 4xx but now gets 302. Fixed by updating test to assert correct new behavior: distribution event shows `qty=1` not 9999. - **Missing imports in moderation_test.go**: Added `"strings"` import after new tests used `strings.Contains`. - **Missing `httptest` import in distribution_test.go**: Added `"net/http/httptest"` for the SSE route test. - **WebFetch returns summaries**: Used `curl` instead to download actual library files. - **Bootstrap Icons needs font files**: Downloaded `bootstrap-icons.woff2` and `.woff` to `web/static/css/fonts/`. - **Leaflet CSS needs images**: Downloaded 5 marker/layer images to `web/static/css/images/`. --- ## 5. Problem Solving **Distribution order-line integrity**: The core problem is that `RecordReturn` and `RecordExchange` accept arbitrary `materialID` parameters that are not checked against what was actually ordered. The fix is to call `GetItemsByOrderID` and build a set of valid material IDs before proceeding. For `RecordReturn`, also check that the return quantity doesn't exceed what was ordered (`oi.Qty`). **Stats authorization**: The `adminOnlyStats` allowlist approach is correct but incomplete. The cleanest fix is to expand the map to include all stats that expose cross-user operational data. An alternative would be a per-stat role map, but the simple expansion is sufficient. **Price in forms**: The `strconv` package is already imported in `materials.go`. Need to add float parsing for `price` in both helper functions. The `floatFormValue` helper doesn't exist — either add it or inline the parse. **Health endpoint**: Simple one-liner change — move `err.Error()` to the log call and return generic body. --- ## 6. All User Messages 1. **`.`** — Session continuation trigger (single period) 2. **First audit remediation request** (5 items: pricing authority, distribution qty forgery, financial receipt, inbox SSE, CSRF tests) — this was in the previous session summary 3. **"make sure there is one README file and it should be inside repo directory"** — deleted outer README 4. **Second audit report** — Full static review report with Blocker (frontend placeholders), High (SSE route, collapsed comments, moderation lifecycle, reissue inventory), Medium (.env) findings. Request: "Produce a detailed patch plan or code diffs that fully addresses these issues, with tests ensuring coverage of all corrected flows." 5. **Third audit report** (current) — New static review with High (distribution return/exchange integrity, stats leak, price in admin UI), Medium (health endpoint, test gaps). Request: "fix all fail ones and make sure all fail ones are fixed and all test cases as met" 6. **"CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY."** — Summary request, no tool calls. --- ## 7. Pending Tasks All items from the third audit report need implementation: 1. **Fix `RecordReturn`** — validate `materialID` against `order_items` for the given `orderID`; also validate returned `qty` doesn't exceed ordered qty 2. **Fix `RecordExchange`** — validate `oldMaterialID` against `order_items` for the given `orderID` 3. **Expand `adminOnlyStats` map** in `internal/handlers/analytics.go` — add `pending-issues`, `backorders`, `moderation-queue`, `course-plans`, `pending-returns` 4. **Add `price` to `materialModelFromForm`** in `internal/handlers/materials.go` 5. **Add `price` to `materialFieldsFromForm`** in `internal/handlers/materials.go` 6. **Add price field to `new.html`** template 7. **Add price field to `edit.html`** template (with current value pre-populated) 8. **Fix health endpoint** in `cmd/server/main.go` — log error, return generic body 9. **Add negative tests for distribution** — material not in order, over-return 10. **Add stats permission tests** — student/non-admin forbidden from sensitive stats --- ## 8. Current Work The third audit report was just submitted. No code changes for the third audit have been made yet. 318	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
319	
320	// adminOnlyStats is the set of stat names that expose sensitive business metrics
321	// and must only be served to admin-role users.
322	var adminOnlyStats = map[string]bool{
323		"total-orders":    true,
324		"active-users":    true,
325		"conversion-rate": true,
326		"repeat-purchase": true,
327	}
328	
329	// DashboardStat handles GET /api/stats/:stat — returns a card-body HTML
330	// partial used by HTMX to populate dashboard KPI cards on page load.
331	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) DashboardStat(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
332		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
333		stat := c.Params("stat")
334	
335		// Sensitive metrics are restricted to admin users only.
336		if adminOnlyStats[stat] && user.Role != "admin" {
337			return c.Status(fiber.StatusForbidden).JSON(fiber.Map{
338				"error": "access denied: admin role required for this metric",
339			})
340		}
341	
342		card, err := h.analyticsService.DashboardStat(stat, user.ID)
343		if err != nil {
344			observability.App.Warn("dashboard stat failed", "stat", stat, "user_id", user.ID, "error", err)
345			// Return a graceful placeholder rather than a hard error so the card
346			// stays visible.
347			card = &services.DashboardStatCard{Count: 0, Label: stat, Icon: "bar-chart", Color: "secondary"}
348		}
349	
350		return c.Render("partials/stat_card_body", fiber.Map{
351			"Count": card.Count,
352			"Label": card.Label,
353			"Icon":  card.Icon,
354			"Color": card.Color,
355		})
356	}

226		// Observability middleware — register before all routes.
227		app.Use(observability.RequestID())
228		app.Use(observability.RequestLogger())
229	
230		// Health check — used by Docker HEALTHCHECK and load balancers.
231		app.Get("/health", func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
232			if err := database.PingContext(c.Context()); err != nil {
233				return c.Status(503).JSON(fiber.Map{"status": "unhealthy", "error": err.Error()})
234			}
235			return c.JSON(fiber.Map{
236				"status": "ok",
237				"uptime": observability.M.Uptime(),
238			})
239		})
240	
241		// Metrics endpoint — admin-only; exposes internal counters.
242		// Registered here but the handler itself re-checks the role so that
243		// the route ordering (before the session middleware) doesn't matter.
244		app.Get("/metrics", authMiddleware.RequireAuth(), middleware.RequireRole("admin"), func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
245			return c.JSON(observability.M.ToJSON())
246		})

All tests pass. Here's a summary of every change made for the third audit round:

**High — Distribution order-line integrity**
- `internal/services/distribution.go`: `RecordReturn` now loads `order_items` for the order and rejects any `materialID` not in that set; also enforces `qty ≤ orderedQty`. `RecordExchange` validates `oldMaterialID` against `order_items` before touching inventory.

**High — Stats API information leak**
- `internal/handlers/analytics.go`: Expanded `adminOnlyStats` to include `pending-issues`, `backorders`, `moderation-queue`, `course-plans`, `pending-returns` (5 new entries).
- `API_tests/helpers_test.go`: Registered the missing `/api/stats/:stat` route in the test app.

**High — Material price not settable via admin UI**
- `internal/handlers/materials.go`: Both `materialModelFromForm` and `materialFieldsFromForm` now parse the `price` form field.
- `web/templates/admin/materials/new.html` and `edit.html`: Added a `Unit Price` input (dollar prefix, `step="0.01"`, pre-populated on edit).

**Medium — Health endpoint leaks raw DB error**
- `cmd/server/main.go`: Error is now logged via `observability.App.Error` and the response body is `{"status":"unhealthy"}` with no `error` key.

**Medium — Test coverage gaps**
- `internal/services/distribution_test.go`: Added `TestDistributionService_RecordReturn_MaterialNotInOrder`, `TestDistributionService_RecordReturn_OverReturn`, and `TestDistributionService_RecordExchange_OldMaterialNotInOrder`.
- `API_tests/permissions_test.go`: Added `TestPermission_Stats_SensitiveStatsForbiddenForStudent`, `TestPermission_Stats_SensitiveStatsForbiddenForInstructor`, and `TestPermission_Stats_AdminCanAccessAllStats` (27 sub-tests total).

- Authentication: **sufficiently covered**

### 8.4 Final Coverage Judgment

- **Partial Pass**
- Major risks covered:

- Major uncovered/misaligned risks:

  - Some object-level and isolation edge cases remain under-tested (e.g., cross-user payment action, private-share permutations).

281	// validTransitions maps (fromStatus, toStatus) pairs that are permitted.
282	var validTransitions = map[string]map[string]bool{
283		"pending_payment": {
284			"pending_shipment": true,
285			"canceled":         true,
286		},
287		"pending_shipment": {
288			"in_transit": true,
289			"canceled":   true,
290		},
291		"in_transit": {
292			"completed": true,
293			"canceled":  true,
294		},

297	// Transition validates and applies an order status transition within a
298	// transaction. It also:
299	//   - Updates auto_close_at: pending_shipment → now+72h, all others → NULL.
300	//   - On cancel: rolls back inventory (available_qty += qty, reserved_qty -= qty).
301	//   - On completed: decrements reserved_qty (items are physically fulfilled).
302	//   - Inserts an order_event row.
303	func (r *OrderRepository) Transition(orderID, actorID int64, toStatus, note string, materialRepo *MaterialRepository) error {
304		tx, err := r.db.Begin()
305		if err != nil {
306			return fmt.Errorf("repository: Transition: begin tx: %w", err)
307		}
308		defer tx.Rollback() //nolint:errcheck
309	
310		// Load current status.
311		var fromStatus string
312		if err := tx.QueryRow(`SELECT status FROM orders WHERE id = ?`, orderID).Scan(&fromStatus); err != nil {
313			if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
314				return fmt.Errorf("repository: Transition: order %d not found", orderID)
315			}
316			return fmt.Errorf("repository: Transition: load status: %w", err)
317		}
318	
319		// Validate transition.
320		allowed, ok := validTransitions[fromStatus]
321		if !ok || !allowed[toStatus] {
322			return fmt.Errorf("repository: Transition: invalid transition %q → %q", fromStatus, toStatus)
323		}
324	
325		// Determine new auto_close_at value.
326		var autoCloseExpr string
327		switch toStatus {
328		case "pending_shipment":
329			autoCloseExpr = "datetime('now', '+72 hours')"
330		default:
331			autoCloseExpr = "NULL"
332		}
333	
334		// Stamp completed_at exactly once — when the order first reaches "completed".
